goal: Establish a consistent study routine for the CPA (Certified Public Accountant) FAR section exam
description: A sustainable daily and weekly routine to cover the Financial Accounting and Reporting syllabus without burnout, focusing on active recall and consistent practice.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Full-time job (9am-6pm), commute time is 45 mins each way (public transit).
baseline.physical_constraints: Mental fatigue high on weekdays after 7pm.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must sleep by 11pm. No studying on Friday evenings.

tasks.description: Review vocabulary and core accounting formulas using spaced repetition software during the morning commute.
tasks.success_criteria: Completion of all 'due' cards in the deck for the day.
tasks.fallback_version: Review 10 'starred' difficult cards only (approx 3 mins).

tasks.description: Read one sub-chapter or watch one lecture video on a new concept during the lunch break.
tasks.success_criteria: Finish one discrete learning module or section.
tasks.fallback_version: Read the summary/key takeaways box of the current chapter.

tasks.description: Complete a set of Multiple Choice Questions related to the day's concept to test immediate understanding.
tasks.success_criteria: Complete 20 MCQs with score > 70%.
tasks.fallback_version: Complete 5 MCQs on the mobile app, regardless of score.

tasks.description: Document incorrect answers from the MCQ session into the error tracking spreadsheet with a brief explanation of why the answer was wrong.
tasks.success_criteria: All incorrect questions from the current session are logged.
tasks.fallback_version: Take a screenshot of incorrect answers to log later.

tasks.description: Complete complex Task-Based Simulations (TBS) that require synthesizing multiple concepts.
tasks.success_criteria: Complete 3 full simulation problems.
tasks.fallback_version: Watch a walkthrough video of 1 simulation problem.

goal: Submit Final CS Capstone Project and Thesis
description: Complete the documentation, code cleanup, and final submission for the 'AI Traffic Control' senior project.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Classes on M/W/F until 2 PM. Part-time work T/Th 1 PM - 5 PM.
baseline.physical_constraints: Cannot type for more than 45 minutes without a stretch break (RSI risk).
baseline.non_negotiables: No coding after 10 PM to ensure sleep quality.

tasks.description: Collect all 15 cited papers and format them into the BibTeX file.
tasks.success_criteria: BibTeX file compiles without errors and matches the citation list.

tasks.description: Write the 1,500-word chapter detailing the algorithm selection and data preprocessing steps.
tasks.success_criteria: Word count > 1400 and section passed through spellcheck.

tasks.description: Clean up the main event loop in `server.py` to remove commented-out debug code and add proper logging.
tasks.success_criteria: Code runs without throwing 'deprecated' warnings and file size is reduced.

tasks.description: Run the auto-doc tool to generate HTML documentation for the endpoints.
tasks.success_criteria: HTML file opens correctly in browser and covers all 4 endpoints.

tasks.description: Record a 5-minute screencast demonstrating the user login and main dashboard features.
tasks.success_criteria: Video file exported to MP4 with clear audio.

goal: Build and Deploy a Personal Portfolio Website with React
description: Create a responsive single-page application portfolio to showcase CS projects, using React.js and deploying to Netlify.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Works 9-5 PM weekdays. Available weekends.
baseline.physical_constraints: Limited focus after 8 PM.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use functional components and hooks (no class components).

tasks.description: Install Node.js, create a new React app using Vite, and strip out boilerplate code.
tasks.success_criteria: Local server runs a blank 'Hello World' page without console errors.

tasks.description: Draw a low-fidelity layout for the Hero section, Projects grid, and Contact footer on paper or Excalidraw.
tasks.success_criteria: A visual plan exists containing at least 3 distinct sections.

tasks.description: Create a responsive Navbar component that links to different page sections.
tasks.success_criteria: Navbar renders at the top of the page and collapses on mobile view.

tasks.description: Create a reusable 'Card' component that accepts props for title, description, image, and GitHub link.
tasks.success_criteria: Component successfully renders data passed via props in a styled box.

tasks.description: Fill the project state with real data from 3 previous university projects and write the 'About Me' text.
tasks.success_criteria: Website displays real text and images instead of placeholders.

tasks.description: Connect GitHub repository to Netlify and trigger the first build.
tasks.success_criteria: Live URL is accessible from a different device.

goal: Optimize Local Machine Learning Model Inference Speed
description: Reduce the latency of a local BERT model's inference by converting it to ONNX format and applying quantization.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Only 8GB VRAM available. CPU is a 6-core processor.
baseline.physical_constraints: High fan noise during testing is disruptive in the shared office.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must maintain at least 95% of original model accuracy.

tasks.description: Run the existing PyTorch model on a set of 100 sample sentences and record the average inference time per sentence.
tasks.success_criteria: A CSV file exists containing 100 latency measurements and a calculated mean.

tasks.description: Use the 'torch.onnx' module to convert the model weights and graph into an .onnx file.
tasks.success_criteria: The file 'model.onnx' is generated and passes the ONNX checker tool.

tasks.description: Run the ONNX Runtime quantization script to convert weights from FP32 to INT8.
tasks.success_criteria: A quantized model file is produced that is approximately 25% of the original file size.

tasks.description: Test the quantized model against the validation dataset to ensure the F1 score hasn't dropped by more than 5%.
tasks.success_criteria: Validation script outputs an accuracy report meeting the 95% retention threshold.

tasks.description: Compare the quantized ONNX inference speed against the baseline recorded in Task 1.
tasks.success_criteria: The final report shows a reduction in average latency (e.g., 2x speedup).

goal: Master Weekly Technical Documentation Writing
description: Improve communication skills by documenting one complex piece of code or system architecture in a clear, accessible README format every week.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Documentation must be completed on Saturday afternoons.
baseline.physical_constraints: Wrist fatigue from coding during the week.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use Markdown. All diagrams must be in Mermaid.js syntax.

tasks.description: Identify one function, class, or logic flow developed during the week that requires explanation.
tasks.success_criteria: A specific code snippet or system component is chosen for documentation.
tasks.fallback_version: Document a simple utility function from a previous project.

tasks.description: Write a 2-paragraph summary explaining the 'Why' behind the chosen topic (problem solved, context).
tasks.success_criteria: A drafted section clearly states the objective and prerequisites.
tasks.fallback_version: Write a 3-bullet point summary of the 'Why'.

tasks.description: Use Mermaid syntax to visualize how data flows through the component.
tasks.success_criteria: The Mermaid code renders a valid diagram showing at least 3 actors/steps.
tasks.fallback_version: List the steps as a numbered list instead of a diagram.

tasks.description: Create a code block showing exactly how a developer would call the function or interact with the system.
tasks.success_criteria: A copy-pasteable code snippet is included that runs without modification.
tasks.fallback_version: Provide a link to the existing test file as an example.

tasks.description: Run the Markdown file through a linter and a spellchecker.
tasks.success_criteria: The file contains zero linting errors and zero spelling mistakes.
tasks.fallback_version: Read the draft aloud once to catch obvious errors.

goal: Secure Personal Linux Server against Unauthorized Access
description: Implement essential security hardening on a new VPS to prevent brute-force attacks and unauthorized root logins.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Using Ubuntu 22.04 LTS. Remote access via SSH only.
baseline.physical_constraints: Risk of locking self out of the server if steps are incorrect.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must disable password authentication entirely. Must use SSH keys.

tasks.description: Create a new user account with a unique name and add it to the 'sudo' group.
tasks.success_criteria: Successful login to the server using the new user and executing 'sudo whoami'.

tasks.description: Generate an Ed25519 key pair locally and transfer the public key to the server.
tasks.success_criteria: User can log in without being prompted for a password.

tasks.description: Edit /etc/ssh/sshd_config to set 'PermitRootLogin' to 'no' and 'PasswordAuthentication' to 'no'.
tasks.success_criteria: SSH service restarts successfully and root login attempts are rejected.

tasks.description: Configure the firewall to block all incoming traffic except for SSH (Port 22).
tasks.success_criteria: 'ufw status' shows 'Status: active' with only Port 22 allowed.

tasks.description: Set up a jail to automatically ban IP addresses after 5 failed login attempts for 24 hours.
tasks.success_criteria: 'fail2ban-client status sshd' shows the service is active and monitoring.

goal: Weekly Open Source Contribution Habit
description: Engage with the open-source community by triaging issues or contributing code to projects I use daily.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Limited to Thursday evenings after work.
baseline.physical_constraints: Imposter syndrome/anxiety regarding public code reviews.
baseline.non_negotiables: No contributions to projects without a Code of Conduct. Keep interactions polite.

tasks.description: Browse the 'Help Wanted' or 'Good First Issue' labels in 3 favorite GitHub repositories.
tasks.success_criteria: One relevant issue is bookmarked or commented on for clarification.
tasks.fallback_version: Read the 'Recently Closed' PRs of a project to understand their standards.

tasks.description: Fork the repository and ensure the project builds and runs all existing tests locally.
tasks.success_criteria: Command 'npm test' or equivalent returns zero failures on the master branch.
tasks.fallback_version: Read the CONTRIBUTING.md file thoroughly instead.

tasks.description: Attempt to reproduce the reported issue by writing a minimal failing test case.
tasks.success_criteria: A local test file exists that consistently fails due to the reported bug.
tasks.fallback_version: Manually confirm the bug by following the steps in the issue description.

tasks.description: Apply a fix for a typo, documentation error, or a minor logic bug identified in Task 3.
tasks.success_criteria: The reproduction test case from Task 3 now passes.
tasks.fallback_version: Submit a documentation-only fix (README or comments).

tasks.description: Draft a clear PR description following the project's template and link to the relevant issue.
tasks.success_criteria: A Pull Request URL is generated on GitHub.
tasks.fallback_version: Save the changes as a draft branch for review next week.

goal: Build a Real-Time Chat App with WebSockets
description: Develop a bidirectional communication app using Node.js and Socket.io that allows multiple users to chat in a single room.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Commute is 30 mins each way.
baseline.physical_constraints: Screen glare issues in the morning; best to code in evening.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must include a 'User is Typing' indicator.

tasks.description: Create a basic HTTP server that serves a static 'index.html' file.
tasks.success_criteria: Navigating to localhost:3000 shows the HTML page.

tasks.description: Install the library and establish a 'connection' event that logs to the server console.
tasks.success_criteria: The console prints 'A user connected' when the browser page is refreshed.

tasks.description: Write the client-side code to emit a 'chat message' event and the server-side code to broadcast it to all users.
tasks.success_criteria: Two different browser windows can send and receive each other's messages.

tasks.description: Listen for the 'keypress' event in the input field and emit a 'typing' event to the server.
tasks.success_criteria: A message saying 'User is typing...' appears in window B when window A is typing.

tasks.description: Use CSS Flexbox to create a scrollable message list and a fixed input bar at the bottom.
tasks.success_criteria: The interface is usable on both mobile and desktop screen widths.

goal: Establish a Habit of Nightly Peer Code Reviews
description: Improve code quality and team knowledge sharing by reviewing at least two pending pull requests every evening.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must be done after the daily stand-up and before finishing the work day.
baseline.physical_constraints: Mental fatigue high after 4 PM.
baseline.non_negotiables: Comments must be constructive and follow the team style guide.

tasks.description: Scan the team's repository for open pull requests and select two that are under 200 lines of code change.
tasks.success_criteria: Two PRs are opened in browser tabs for review.
tasks.fallback_version: Select only one small PR or a documentation-only PR.

tasks.description: Read through the changed files to ensure the business logic matches the requirements stated in the linked ticket.
tasks.success_criteria: Understanding of the code flow is confirmed without needing to ask the author.
tasks.fallback_version: Focus only on checking for obvious syntax errors or missing edge cases.

tasks.description: Check that the PR includes new unit tests and that all CI/CD checks have passed.
tasks.success_criteria: Verified that test coverage has not decreased.
tasks.fallback_version: Check only that the build status is green.

tasks.description: Provide specific feedback on variable naming, potential refactors, or bugs, then submit the review.
tasks.success_criteria: The 'Submit Review' button is clicked and the author is notified.
tasks.fallback_version: Approve the PR with a simple 'LGTM' if no issues are found in a quick scan.

goal: Implement a Production-Ready CI/CD Pipeline for a Microservice
description: Automate the build, test, and deployment process using GitHub Actions and AWS ECR.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Service must stay online during deployment (Zero-downtime).
baseline.physical_constraints: Restricted access to production environment during weekends.
baseline.non_negotiables: Secrets must be stored in GitHub Secrets, never in code.

tasks.description: Create a .github/workflows/main.yml file that triggers on push to the main branch and builds the Docker image.
tasks.success_criteria: The GitHub Action successfully builds a Docker image on a fresh push.

tasks.description: Add a step to the workflow to run the service's test suite inside the container.
tasks.success_criteria: The workflow fails if any unit test fails.

tasks.description: Set up OIDC or IAM credentials to allow GitHub Actions to push images to AWS ECR.
tasks.success_criteria: The workflow successfully logs into the private ECR registry.

tasks.description: Add the 'docker push' command to the workflow with tags for versioning and 'latest'.
tasks.success_criteria: A new image tag appears in the AWS Console after the workflow finishes.

tasks.description: Add a final step to trigger a rolling update on the ECS cluster using the new image.
tasks.success_criteria: The staging URL reflects the most recent code changes without manual intervention.

goal: Solve One LeetCode Problem Weekly in Rust
description: Gain proficiency in Rust by solving algorithmic problems, focusing on ownership and memory safety concepts.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Limited to Sunday mornings.
baseline.physical_constraints: Rust's compiler messages are dense; requires high focus.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use idiomatic Rust (no 'unsafe' blocks).

tasks.description: Choose a 'Medium' problem from the Top Interview 150 list that involves String manipulation or Vectors.
tasks.success_criteria: A specific problem URL is selected and the requirements are understood.
tasks.fallback_version: Choose an 'Easy' problem instead.

tasks.description: Attempt the solution and resolve all compilation errors related to ownership or lifetimes.
tasks.success_criteria: The code compiles successfully using 'cargo check'.
tasks.fallback_version: Consult the Rust documentation or 'The Book' for the specific error encountered.

tasks.description: Run the code against the LeetCode test suite and iterate until it passes.
tasks.success_criteria: The submission is accepted on the first or second attempt.
tasks.fallback_version: Read the solution in the LeetCode discussion and type it out manually.

tasks.description: Refactor the working solution to use more functional patterns like .map(), .filter(), or .collect().
tasks.success_criteria: The refactored code passes tests and looks cleaner.
tasks.fallback_version: Watch a 5-minute video on 'Idiomatic Rust' for that specific problem type.

goal: Implement a Balanced Binary Search Tree (AVL Tree) from Scratch
description: Deepen understanding of self-balancing trees by implementing a BST with rotation logic to maintain O(log n) height.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Completed in a local IDE using Java.
baseline.physical_constraints: Visualizing rotations can be mentally taxing; best done on a whiteboard first.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must include search, insert, and delete operations.

tasks.description: Define the Node class with left, right, and height attributes.
tasks.success_criteria: Node class compiles and can be instantiated with an integer value.

tasks.description: Implement a recursive insert function that places nodes correctly without balancing.
tasks.success_criteria: An in-order traversal of the tree prints values in sorted order.

tasks.description: Write the helper functions for 'leftRotate' and 'rightRotate'.
tasks.success_criteria: Manual test shows the tree structure changes correctly while maintaining BST properties.

tasks.description: Integrate the height check and rotations into the insert function to handle the 4 imbalance cases.
tasks.success_criteria: The height of the tree after 100 random inserts is less than or equal to 1.44 * log2(100).

tasks.description: Implement the search operation and the delete operation with post-deletion balancing.
tasks.success_criteria: Searching for a non-existent value returns null; deleting a node keeps the tree balanced.

goal: Daily Review of Official Documentation for a New Framework
description: Stay updated with modern web standards by reading the official documentation for Next.js 15 for 20 minutes every morning.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during the morning commute or before the first work meeting.
baseline.physical_constraints: Reading on a small mobile screen may be difficult for complex diagrams.
baseline.non_negotiables: Focus on the 'App Router' features specifically.

tasks.description: Pick one specific chapter (e.g., 'Data Fetching' or 'Server Actions') from the official sidebar.
tasks.success_criteria: The correct documentation page is loaded in the browser.
tasks.fallback_version: Re-read the 'Getting Started' page for a quick refresher.

tasks.description: Read the section thoroughly, paying attention to code examples and warning boxes.
tasks.success_criteria: All sub-headers on the page have been read.
tasks.fallback_version: Skim the 'Key Takeaways' or summary bullets if time is short.

tasks.description: Copy one code example into a personal note-taking app and add comments explaining what each line does.
tasks.success_criteria: One annotated code snippet is saved in the 'Next.js 15' folder.
tasks.fallback_version: Bookmark the specific code example for later review.

tasks.description: Identify one way the new concept differs from previous versions or other frameworks.
tasks.success_criteria: A short comparison sentence is written down.
tasks.fallback_version: Think of one question about the concept to look up tomorrow.

goal: Daily Practice of Refactoring Legacy Code
description: Improve code maintainability skills by taking a small, messy code snippet daily and applying clean code principles like SOLID or DRY.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during the final 30 minutes of the workday.
baseline.physical_constraints: Evening mental fog makes complex architectural changes difficult.
baseline.non_negotiables: The code must remain functional at the end of every session (no broken builds).

tasks.description: Identify a function or class in the current project with a cyclomatic complexity higher than 5.
tasks.success_criteria: A target code block is identified and copied into a local scratchpad file.
tasks.fallback_version: Use a pre-selected 'bad code' example from a public repository like 'Refactoring Guru'.

tasks.description: Create a suite of 3-5 unit tests that describe the current behavior of the snippet to prevent regressions.
tasks.success_criteria: All tests pass against the original, un-refactored code.
tasks.fallback_version: Write 1 test for the most common input path only.

tasks.description: Apply a specific pattern such as 'Extract Method', 'Replace Conditional with Polymorphism', or 'Rename Variable'.
tasks.success_criteria: Code structure is simplified while maintaining identical output behavior.
tasks.fallback_version: Only rename variables to be more descriptive without changing structure.

tasks.description: Run the tests from Task 2 and remove any temporary comments or dead code.
tasks.success_criteria: Tests pass and no unused variables remain in the refactored code.
tasks.fallback_version: Manually verify the main output once if the test runner is too slow.

tasks.description: Write a single sentence in a dev log explaining which Clean Code principle was applied.
tasks.success_criteria: A new entry exists in the daily refactoring log.
tasks.fallback_version: Bookmark the refactoring pattern used for later reading.

goal: Deploy a Secured Dockerized Node.js API
description: Containerize an existing Express.js application and implement industry-standard security hardening for the production image.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use the official Node:alpine base image.
baseline.physical_constraints: Limited bandwidth for downloading large Docker layers.
baseline.non_negotiables: The container must not run as a root user. All secrets must be passed via environment variables.

tasks.description: Write a Dockerfile with separate 'build' and 'production' stages to minimize image size.
tasks.success_criteria: The final image contains only production dependencies and is under 200MB.

tasks.description: Add commands in the Dockerfile to create a system user and set the 'USER' instruction.
tasks.success_criteria: Running 'docker exec [container] whoami' returns a name other than 'root'.

tasks.description: Add a HEALTHCHECK instruction to the Dockerfile to monitor the API's 'up' status.
tasks.success_criteria: 'docker ps' shows the container status as '(healthy)'.

tasks.description: Execute 'docker scout' or 'trivy' to identify vulnerabilities in the image layers.
tasks.success_criteria: The scan report shows zero 'High' or 'Critical' vulnerabilities.

tasks.description: Tag the final hardened image and push it to a local Docker registry for testing.
tasks.success_criteria: The image is successfully stored in the registry and can be pulled back.

goal: Weekly Deep-Dive into Distributed Systems Research
description: Build a high-level understanding of fundamental distributed systems papers (e.g., Paxos, Raft, Dynamo) through weekly reading and summarization.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Completed on Saturday mornings before 11 AM.
baseline.physical_constraints: Paper vocabulary is dense; requires an external dictionary/AI for terminology.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must maintain a permanent bibliography in Obsidian or Notion.

tasks.description: Choose one paper from the 'Distributed Systems Reading List' (e.g., Lamport's Logical Clocks).
tasks.success_criteria: The PDF is downloaded and a new entry is created in the bibliography.
tasks.fallback_version: Select a technical blog post summarizing a paper instead.

tasks.description: Read the first two sections and write down the primary problem the paper aims to solve.
tasks.success_criteria: The 'Problem Statement' is clearly articulated in 2-3 sentences.
tasks.fallback_version: Read only the abstract and look at the diagrams.

tasks.description: Draw a small flow diagram or state machine representing the core algorithm mentioned (e.g., leader election).
tasks.success_criteria: A visual representation of the logic exists in the study notes.
tasks.fallback_version: Copy a diagram from the paper and add labels in my own words.

tasks.description: Write a 500-word summary including the solution, its limitations, and one modern use case.
tasks.success_criteria: Summary is saved in the permanent bibliography with consistent tags.
tasks.fallback_version: Write a 3-bullet point 'TL;DR' summary.

goal: Develop a Basic Software Renderer in C++
description: Build a line-and-triangle renderer from scratch without using APIs like OpenGL or DirectX, learning the mathematics of 3D projection.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must run on a standard Linux/Windows terminal or output to a .ppm file.
baseline.physical_constraints: Geometry math is mentally draining; 45-minute maximum focus per session.
baseline.non_negotiables: No external graphics libraries allowed for the core math/rendering.

tasks.description: Create a simple C++ class to represent a pixel array and save it as a .ppm image file.
tasks.success_criteria: A valid 'test.ppm' image is generated that opens in an image viewer.

tasks.description: Write the function to draw a line between two coordinates (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) in the buffer.
tasks.success_criteria: The generated image displays a diagonal line without gaps.

tasks.description: Implement the perspective projection matrix math to transform 3D vertices into 2D screen coordinates.
tasks.success_criteria: The program successfully transforms a set of 8 cube vertices into 2D space.

tasks.description: Connect the projected vertices using the line algorithm from Task 2 to render a 3D wireframe cube.
tasks.success_criteria: The final image clearly depicts a 3D cube structure.

tasks.description: Implement a basic scanline or half-space algorithm to fill in the triangles of the cube faces.
tasks.success_criteria: The cube appears as solid shapes rather than just lines.

goal: Daily Practice of SQL Window Functions
description: Master advanced SQL analytics by solving one complex query challenge daily using window functions like RANK, LEAD, and LAG.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Practice on a local PostgreSQL instance or LeetCode SQL section.
baseline.physical_constraints: Limited focus during late-night sessions.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the 'OVER()' clause in every solution.

tasks.description: Find a problem that requires calculating running totals, moving averages, or ranking within partitions.
tasks.success_criteria: A specific query prompt and schema are identified.
tasks.fallback_version: Re-solve a 'Basic' window function problem from the previous week.

tasks.description: Write the SQL query to solve the problem, ensuring correct usage of PARTITION BY and ORDER BY within the window.
tasks.success_criteria: The query returns a result set without syntax errors.
tasks.fallback_version: Write only the 'SELECT' and 'OVER' part of the query as a skeleton.

tasks.description: Manually check the first 5 rows of the output to ensure the window logic (e.g., the rank or the lag) is correct.
tasks.success_criteria: The output matches the expected analytical result for the sample data.
tasks.fallback_version: Use the 'EXPLAIN' command to just verify the query execution plan.

tasks.description: Check if adding an index or changing the window partition order improves the query cost.
tasks.success_criteria: The query is refined to its most efficient version.
tasks.fallback_version: Read one article on SQL window function performance optimization.

goal: Build a Custom Layer 7 Load Balancer in Python
description: Develop a tool that distributes incoming HTTP traffic across multiple backend servers using a round-robin algorithm to understand proxying and socket programming.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Limited to Python 3.10 standard library where possible.
baseline.physical_constraints: Debugging network sockets is mentally intensive; session cap of 60 minutes.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the 'socket' library. Must handle at least 3 concurrent backend connections.

tasks.description: Create a configuration file or list containing the IP addresses and port numbers of 3 local test servers.
tasks.success_criteria: A JSON or list object is correctly initialized and accessible by the main script.

tasks.description: Write a generator function that yields the next backend server address in a circular fashion.
tasks.success_criteria: Calling the function 4 times returns server A, B, C, and then A again.

tasks.description: Write the socket code to receive a request from a client and open a new socket to forward the raw data to a backend.
tasks.success_criteria: The backend server receives the exact HTTP headers sent by the client.

tasks.description: Implement the return path to send the data received from the backend back to the original client socket.
tasks.success_criteria: The client (browser or curl) receives a valid HTTP 200 response from the backend through the load balancer.

tasks.description: Log the timestamp, client IP, and target backend server for every processed request to the console.
tasks.success_criteria: Console shows a clear history of which requests went to which servers.

goal: Daily Cyber-Security CTF Problem Solving
description: Strengthen penetration testing skills by completing one 'Capture The Flag' challenge daily, focusing on web exploitation and cryptography.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done at 8 PM after dinner.
baseline.physical_constraints: Evening screen fatigue; requires blue light filter enabled.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must document the exact steps of the exploit (the 'kill chain').

tasks.description: Select one 'Easy' or 'Medium' challenge from PicoCTF or Hack The Box based on the current weekly theme.
tasks.success_criteria: A challenge instance is launched and accessible via VPN/browser.
tasks.fallback_version: Re-solve a previous 'Easy' challenge from memory to reinforce speed.

tasks.description: Use tools like Nmap, Gobuster, or browser DevTools to identify open ports or hidden directories.
tasks.success_criteria: At least one potential vulnerability or entry point is identified.
tasks.fallback_version: Read the first hint provided by the platform to guide the scan.

tasks.description: Apply a specific technique (e.g., SQL Injection, XSS) to bypass authentication or retrieve hidden data.
tasks.success_criteria: The 'flag' string is found and submitted to the platform.
tasks.fallback_version: Watch the first 3 minutes of a video walkthrough if stuck for over 30 minutes.

tasks.description: Record the commands used and the logic behind the successful exploit in a markdown file.
tasks.success_criteria: The markdown file contains a reproducible set of steps.
tasks.fallback_version: Save only the final command that captured the flag.

goal: Build a Blockchain Prototype in Go
description: Create a simplified immutable ledger that implements cryptographic hashing, block linking, and a proof-of-work mining mechanism.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use standard Go libraries for crypto/sha256.
baseline.physical_constraints: New to Go concurrency patterns; requires careful reading of documentation.
baseline.non_negotiables: Each block must be cryptographically linked to the previous one. Data must be immutable once mined.

tasks.description: Create a struct in Go representing a Block with fields for Index, Timestamp, Data, PreviousHash, Hash, and Nonce.
tasks.success_criteria: The Block struct can be instantiated and printed to the console.

tasks.description: Write a function that concatenates block fields into a string and returns its SHA-256 hash.
tasks.success_criteria: Changing a single character in the data field results in a completely different hash output.

tasks.description: Write a function that hardcodes and returns the first block (index 0) of the chain.
tasks.success_criteria: A Blockchain slice is initialized containing exactly one block with 'PreviousHash' set to '0'.

tasks.description: Create a Proof-of-Work function that increments a Nonce until the resulting hash starts with a specific number of zeros (difficulty).
tasks.success_criteria: The program successfully finds a hash that meets a difficulty of 4 zeros within 1 minute.

tasks.description: Write a loop that iterates through the chain to verify that each block's 'PreviousHash' matches the actual hash of the preceding block.
tasks.success_criteria: The function returns 'true' for a valid chain and 'false' if a previous block's data is manually altered.

goal: Weekly Technical Mock Interview Practice
description: Simulate a real-world software engineering interview environment every Saturday to improve technical communication and live coding performance.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Scheduled for Saturday mornings at 10 AM.
baseline.physical_constraints: High performance anxiety; requires 10 minutes of breathing exercises beforehand.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must code in a 'blank' environment without auto-complete or AI assistance.

tasks.description: Connect with a peer on Pramp or use a randomized generator to get a 'Medium' difficulty behavioral or technical prompt.
tasks.success_criteria: A specific, unfamiliar problem is ready for solving.
tasks.fallback_version: Use a familiar problem from a month ago to practice 'perfect' delivery.

tasks.description: Spend 5 minutes asking clarifying questions about inputs, edge cases, and constraints before touching the keyboard.
tasks.success_criteria: A clear understanding of the 'definition of done' for the problem is verbalized.
tasks.fallback_version: Write down the constraints as bullet points in the editor.

tasks.description: Solve the problem while explaining the thought process out loud to the interviewer or a recording device.
tasks.success_criteria: The problem is solved and the code passes all dry-run test cases.
tasks.fallback_version: Solve the problem silently first, then record a 2-minute summary of the solution.

tasks.description: Watch the recording or review peer feedback to identify 2 specific areas for improvement (e.g., filler words, slow Big O analysis).
tasks.success_criteria: Two actionable 'To-Do' items are added to the career preparation journal.
tasks.fallback_version: Read a 'Model Solution' for the problem and note one difference in logic.

goal: Set Up a Monitoring Dashboard for a Home Server
description: Deploy a visual monitoring stack to track CPU, Memory, and Disk health of a Raspberry Pi or local PC in real-time.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use Prometheus and Grafana. Must run as Docker containers.
baseline.physical_constraints: Local network firewall issues might require port-forwarding troubleshooting.
baseline.non_negotiables: Dashboard must be password protected.

tasks.description: Pull the official Prometheus image and mount a basic 'prometheus.yml' config file.
tasks.success_criteria: Accessing localhost:9090 shows the Prometheus Expression Browser.

tasks.description: Install and start Node Exporter on the host machine to collect hardware-level metrics.
tasks.success_criteria: The Prometheus 'targets' page shows Node Exporter as 'UP'.

tasks.description: Start a Grafana container and map its internal port 3000 to the host.
tasks.success_criteria: The Grafana login page is reachable via a web browser.

tasks.description: Inside Grafana, add the local Prometheus container address as a 'Prometheus' data source.
tasks.success_criteria: The 'Test Connection' button returns a 'Data source is working' message.

tasks.description: Import the 'Node Exporter Full' dashboard (ID 1860) to visualize the host metrics.
tasks.success_criteria: A live-updating dashboard with at least 5 different charts is visible.

goal: Build a Pathfinding Visualizer for Dijkstra and A*
description: Create a web-based tool that visualizes how Dijkstra's and A* algorithms find the shortest path on a 2D grid with obstacles.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must be built using Vanilla JavaScript or React.
baseline.physical_constraints: Geometric coordinate logic can be confusing; requires a clear mental state.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must allow the user to click-and-drag to draw walls. Must show the 'visited' nodes animation.

tasks.description: Create a 2D array and render it as a grid of <div> elements with 'Start' and 'End' nodes.
tasks.success_criteria: A 20x20 grid is visible in the browser with one green node and one red node.

tasks.description: Add event listeners to grid cells to toggle a 'wall' state on mouse click or drag.
tasks.success_criteria: Cells turn black when clicked, and the underlying data array updates to 'isWall: true'.

tasks.description: Write the Dijkstra algorithm logic that calculates distances and tracks the 'previousNode' for backtracking.
tasks.success_criteria: The algorithm returns an ordered list of visited nodes and the shortest path nodes.

tasks.description: Use CSS transitions and 'setTimeout' to change the background color of visited nodes sequentially.
tasks.success_criteria: The grid 'fills' from the start node outward until it hits the end node or walls.

tasks.description: Modify the logic to include Manhattan distance (h-cost) to turn the Dijkstra search into an A* search.
tasks.success_criteria: A* finds the path using significantly fewer node visits than Dijkstra on an open grid.

goal: Daily Review of System Design Patterns
description: Internalize distributed system patterns (Circuit Breaker, Idempotency, CQRS) through daily targeted reading.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Performed during the lunch break (12 PM - 1 PM).
baseline.physical_constraints: Limited to reading on a tablet or phone.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must explain the concept to a 'rubber duck' (out loud) to verify comprehension.

tasks.description: Pick one pattern from the 'Cloud Design Patterns' catalog (e.g., Retry, Throttling).
tasks.success_criteria: One specific pattern document is opened.
tasks.fallback_version: Re-read the 'Overview' of a previous pattern.

tasks.description: Identify the Problem, Solution, and at least 2 potential drawbacks of the pattern.
tasks.success_criteria: The 'Problem' and 'Solution' are summarized in personal notes.
tasks.fallback_version: Read the 'Example' section only.

tasks.description: Explain the pattern out loud as if explaining it to a junior engineer.
tasks.success_criteria: Explanation is completed without hesitating on the core mechanism.
tasks.fallback_version: Write a 3-sentence summary instead.

tasks.description: Find one modern tool that implements this pattern (e.g., Netflix Hystrix for Circuit Breaker).
tasks.success_criteria: One real-world tool or library is identified and logged.
tasks.fallback_version: Skip if the pattern is strictly conceptual.

goal: Build a Multi-Threaded Port Scanner in C
description: Create a high-performance C program that scans a range of TCP ports using POSIX threads to understand concurrency and networking.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must compile with GCC on Linux.
baseline.physical_constraints: C pointer errors (Segfaults) are frustrating; requires an environment with GDB installed.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use 'pthread' library. Must handle 'Connection Refused' errors gracefully.

tasks.description: Write a basic C loop that tries to 'connect()' to a range of ports (1-1024) one by one.
tasks.success_criteria: The program correctly identifies 'open' vs 'closed' ports on localhost.

tasks.description: Refactor the code to create 10 worker threads that print their unique Thread IDs.
tasks.success_criteria: The program outputs 10 distinct thread IDs upon execution.

tasks.description: Create a shared 'port_counter' and a mutex to ensure threads pick unique ports to scan without duplication.
tasks.success_criteria: All 1024 ports are scanned, and no port is scanned twice.

tasks.description: Set the socket to non-blocking mode and use 'select()' to handle timeouts for slow-responding ports.
tasks.success_criteria: The scanner doesn't hang indefinitely on filtered/firewalled ports.

tasks.description: Run the scanner against a local target and measure the total execution time compared to Task 1.
tasks.success_criteria: The threaded scanner is at least 5x faster than the sequential version.

goal: Weekly Practice of Writing Technical Blog Posts
description: Improve technical writing by publishing one short, high-quality blog post about a concept learned during the week.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Drafting on Friday, publishing on Sunday.
baseline.physical_constraints: Typing fatigue; recommends using a mechanical keyboard.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must include at least one code snippet and one visual aid.

tasks.description: Review the week's study notes and select one topic that was difficult to understand at first.
tasks.success_criteria: One clear topic title is chosen.
tasks.fallback_version: Write a 'Links of the Week' post with 3 useful resources found.

tasks.description: Write the core section of the post explaining the breakthrough or the logic in simple terms.
tasks.success_criteria: A draft exists with at least 300 words of content.
tasks.fallback_version: Write only the bulleted outline of the concept.

tasks.description: Write and test a minimal, copy-pasteable code example that illustrates the concept.
tasks.success_criteria: Code snippet is formatted correctly and confirmed to run.
tasks.fallback_version: Use an existing code snippet from a previous task.

tasks.description: Review for flow, add a feature image, and publish to Hashnode or Dev.to.
tasks.success_criteria: The post is live and the URL is shared on one social platform.
tasks.fallback_version: Save as a draft for two weeks instead of publishing.

goal: Migrate a Monolithic App to Serverless Functions
description: Extract two high-traffic endpoints from a monolithic Express app and redeploy them as AWS Lambda functions.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use the Serverless Framework or AWS SAM.
baseline.physical_constraints: IAM permissions can be a 'rabbit hole'; set a hard stop for troubleshooting.
baseline.non_negotiables: The database connection must be shared (RDS/DynamoDB).

tasks.description: Identify the two endpoints with the highest latency and map their external dependencies.
tasks.success_criteria: Two specific routes (e.g., /get-user, /upload-image) are selected.

tasks.description: Setup a serverless.yml file and configure the provider as AWS with the correct region.
tasks.success_criteria: 'serverless deploy' (on an empty project) returns a success code.

tasks.description: Rewrite the Express route logic into a standalone handler(event, context) function.
tasks.success_criteria: The function executes successfully using 'serverless invoke local'.

tasks.description: Map the HTTP events in serverless.yml to the new Lambda functions.
tasks.success_criteria: Deploying produces a public URL that triggers the Lambda function.

tasks.description: Update the frontend or client to point to the new Lambda URL for the specific endpoints.
tasks.success_criteria: Client logs show requests successfully hitting the AWS Lambda endpoint.

goal: Master the Basics of Assembly Language (x86-64)
description: Learn to write and debug low-level programs by implementing basic arithmetic and control flow directly in x86-64 assembly.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use NASM assembler and GCC for linking on a Linux environment.
baseline.physical_constraints: Mental exhaustion occurs quickly when tracking register states; sessions limited to 45 minutes.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the System V AMD64 ABI calling convention for function calls.

tasks.description: Write a program using system calls (sys_write and sys_exit) to print a string to the terminal.
tasks.success_criteria: The binary executes and outputs the correct string to the console.

tasks.description: Create a function that takes two integers via registers, adds them, and returns the result in RAX.
tasks.success_criteria: A small C wrapper correctly prints the sum returned by the assembly function.

tasks.description: Write an assembly routine that calculates the sum of the first N integers using a loop and conditional jumps (cmp/jne).
tasks.success_criteria: The program correctly calculates the sum for N=10 and N=100.

tasks.description: Use GDB to step through the loop line-by-line and observe how the values in RDI and RAX change.
tasks.success_criteria: Observed and logged the register values at 3 different points in the loop execution.

tasks.description: Implement a function that uses the stack for local variable storage, correctly managing the base pointer (RBP).
tasks.success_criteria: The function returns the correct value and doesn't crash the program upon returning.

goal: Weekly Audit of Personal Cloud Security Settings
description: Ensure personal cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP) remains secure by performing a weekly manual check of IAM roles and security groups.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Performed on Monday mornings before starting deep work.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires high attention to detail to spot misconfigurations.
baseline.non_negotiables: Any '0.0.0.0/0' inbound rules on non-web ports must be flagged and removed immediately.

tasks.description: Check the list of active IAM access keys and identify any that have not been used in the last 30 days.
tasks.success_criteria: Stale keys are identified and deactivated.
tasks.fallback_version: Check only the keys associated with the 'Admin' group.

tasks.description: Review all active Security Groups for open ports, specifically looking for SSH (22) or RDP (3389) open to the world.
tasks.success_criteria: A list of all inbound rules for all active instances is reviewed.
tasks.fallback_version: Check only the security groups for 'Production' tagged instances.

tasks.description: Verify that no S3 buckets are set to 'Public Access' unless explicitly intended for static hosting.
tasks.success_criteria: Public access settings for all buckets are confirmed in the console.
tasks.fallback_version: Check the 'S3 Public Access Block' account-level setting only.

tasks.description: Log the findings and any remediation steps taken into a private security journal.
tasks.success_criteria: A new entry exists in the security audit log for the current date.
tasks.fallback_version: Email a quick summary of the check to myself.

goal: Build a Custom Web Scraper for Market Analysis
description: Create a Python-based scraper that extracts pricing data from a target e-commerce site and saves it to a structured JSON format.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use BeautifulSoup4 and Requests libraries.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires sitting at a desk for long periods; remember to stand every 20 mins.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must respect the site's robots.txt and implement a 2-second delay between requests.

tasks.description: Use browser DevTools to identify the HTML tags and classes used for product names and prices.
tasks.success_criteria: A list of 3 CSS selectors (name, price, availability) is documented.

tasks.description: Write a Python script that fetches the HTML of a single product page and prints the raw content.
tasks.success_criteria: The script returns a 200 OK status code and the HTML body is visible in the terminal.

tasks.description: Use BeautifulSoup to parse the HTML and isolate the specific product name and price strings.
tasks.success_criteria: The script prints a clean dictionary: {'name': '...', 'price': '...'}.

tasks.description: Wrap the extraction logic in a loop that iterates through a list of 10 URLs with a 2-second sleep between each.
tasks.success_criteria: The script successfully fetches data from all 10 URLs without being blocked.

tasks.description: Modify the script to save the final list of dictionaries to a file named 'market_data.json'.
tasks.success_criteria: A valid 'market_data.json' file exists and contains data for all 10 products.

goal: Daily Review of Distributed Systems Latency Numbers
description: Internalize 'Latency Numbers Every Programmer Should Know' (e.g., L1 cache vs. Disk seek) through daily recall.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Review done during the morning commute or first cup of coffee.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires visualizing scale (nanoseconds to seconds).
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to state the order of magnitude for each number.

tasks.description: Try to list the latencies for: L1 cache, Main Memory, SSD random read, and a round-trip within a data center from memory.
tasks.success_criteria: At least 4 latency numbers are written down from memory.
tasks.fallback_version: Read the 'Latency Numbers' chart and highlight 3 specific rows.

tasks.description: Compare the recalled numbers against the official interactive chart and note any errors in magnitude.
tasks.success_criteria: Verified the accuracy of all recalled numbers.
tasks.fallback_version: Copy the correct numbers into a notebook once.

tasks.description: Think of one system design scenario where these numbers matter (e.g., why caching is necessary for database queries).
tasks.success_criteria: A one-sentence scenario is identified and verbalized.
tasks.fallback_version: Read one 'Case Study' blurb on why memory is faster than disk.

goal: Implement a Generic Linked List in Rust
description: Understand Rust's memory management by implementing a singly linked list that can hold any data type using generics and Box pointers.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must not use 'unsafe' blocks. Must follow the 'Learning Rust With Entirely Too Many Linked Lists' methodology.
baseline.physical_constraints: Wrestling with the borrow checker can lead to frustration; take a break if stuck on a compiler error for 15 mins.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must implement push, pop, and peek methods.

tasks.task: Define the Node and List Structs
tasks.description: Create the basic structure using 'Option<Box<Node<T>>>' to handle null pointers safely.
tasks.success_criteria: The code compiles with an empty list definition.

tasks.description: Write the logic to add a new element to the front of the list, transferring ownership of the current head.
tasks.success_criteria: A test case successfully adds 3 elements to the list without compiler errors.

tasks.description: Write the logic to remove the head of the list and return its value, using 'Option::take()'.
tasks.success_criteria: A test case pushes 2 elements and pops them back in the correct order.

tasks.description: Implement methods to return immutable and mutable references to the head value without removing it.
tasks.success_criteria: A test case can modify the head value of the list in place.

tasks.description: Manually implement the Drop trait to iteratively clean up nodes and prevent stack overflow from recursive drops.
tasks.success_criteria: The list can hold 1,000,000 elements and drop them without a stack overflow.

goal: Build a Real-Time Distributed Key-Value Store
description: Develop a simplified version of Redis that supports concurrent reads/writes and basic networking using Go and TCP sockets.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must run on a local network. Using Go 1.21+.
baseline.physical_constraints: High cognitive load when debugging race conditions; sessions limited to 90 minutes.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be thread-safe. Must support 'SET', 'GET', and 'DEL' commands.

tasks.description: Create a thread-safe wrapper around a Go map using sync.RWMutex to store string keys and values.
tasks.success_criteria: A unit test confirms concurrent writes do not cause a panic.

tasks.description: Write a parser for a subset of the REdis Serialization Protocol (RESP) to handle incoming command strings.
tasks.success_criteria: Parser correctly identifies 'SET key value' from a raw byte stream.

tasks.description: Implement a network listener that accepts multiple concurrent TCP connections and spawns a goroutine for each.
tasks.success_criteria: Server can maintain 5 idle client connections simultaneously.

tasks.description: Link the TCP listener and the parser to the storage engine to perform real data operations.
tasks.success_criteria: Sending 'SET name gemini' via telnet allows a subsequent 'GET name' to return 'gemini'.

tasks.description: Write a background worker that appends every write command to a 'log.aof' file for crash recovery.
tasks.success_criteria: Restarting the server restores the previous state from the .aof file.

goal: Daily Technical Paper Skimming Habit
description: Stay at the forefront of AI and CS by skimming the latest pre-prints on arXiv every morning.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Performed while commuting or during breakfast.
baseline.physical_constraints: Mobile device reading; focus on figures and abstracts.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must save at least one paper per week to the 'Deep Read' list.

tasks.description: Open the arXiv 'recent' page for Computer Science and scroll through the last 24 hours of titles.
tasks.success_criteria: At least 3 interesting titles are identified.
tasks.fallback_version: Read a curated newsletter (e.g., Import AI) instead of the raw feed.

tasks.description: Read the abstract of the most relevant paper and look at 'Figure 1' or the 'Results' table.
tasks.success_criteria: The core contribution of the paper is understood and summarized in one sentence.
tasks.fallback_version: Read only the 'Conclusion' section.

tasks.description: Identify one cited work in the paper that is a foundational precursor to the current research.
tasks.success_criteria: The name of one foundational paper is noted down.
tasks.fallback_version: Skip if the current paper is self-contained.

goal: Optimize a Web Application's Critical Rendering Path
description: Improve the Lighthouse performance score of a personal site from <70 to >90 by optimizing asset loading.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Testing on a simulated 'Slow 4G' mobile connection.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires multiple browser restarts for clean cache testing.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must not remove any existing visual features or images.

tasks.description: Run a Lighthouse audit and identify the top 3 'opportunities' (e.g., render-blocking resources).
tasks.success_criteria: A baseline report is saved with a score and specific metrics (LCP, CLS).

tasks.description: Convert all images to WebP format and implement 'loading=lazy' for off-screen assets.
tasks.success_criteria: Network tab shows smaller payloads and deferred loading for bottom-of-page images.

tasks.description: Inline the critical CSS required for the above-the-fold content and defer the rest using a media='print' trick or async load.
tasks.success_criteria: Lighthouse 'First Contentful Paint' (FCP) improves by at least 200ms.

tasks.description: Enable Gzip or Brotli compression on the server and ensure all JS/CSS files are minified.
tasks.success_criteria: Response headers for .js files show 'Content-Encoding: br' or 'gzip'.

tasks.description: Perform the final Lighthouse audit to confirm the new score meets the target of >90.
tasks.success_criteria: Score is confirmed as 90+ across 3 consecutive runs.

goal: Weekly Documentation of Home Network Architecture
description: Maintain an accurate technical map of all devices, static IPs, and VLANs in the home lab to assist in future troubleshooting.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Sunday afternoon activity.
baseline.physical_constraints: May require physically checking labels on hardware (routers/switches).
baseline.non_negotiables: All documentation must be stored in a centralized wiki or git repo.

tasks.description: Login to the router and check the 'Connected Devices' list against the existing documentation.
tasks.success_criteria: Any new MAC addresses are identified and assigned a descriptive name.
tasks.fallback_version: Check only the static IP assignments.

tasks.description: Modify the Mermaid.js or Draw.io diagram to reflect any changes in VLAN tagging or port forwarding.
tasks.success_criteria: The diagram matches the current physical and logical cabling.
tasks.fallback_version: Hand-sketch changes on a notepad to be digitized later.

tasks.description: Record the current firmware versions for the gateway, switch, and access points.
tasks.success_criteria: A table in the wiki is updated with current versions and 'Last Checked' date.
tasks.fallback_version: Only check the main gateway for security updates.

goal: Build a CLI Tool for Automated Backups to AWS S3
description: Create a Python script that zips a specified local directory and uploads it to a private S3 bucket with a timestamped filename.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use Boto3 library. Running on Windows/Linux.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires managing AWS credentials securely.
baseline.non_negotiables: Backups must be encrypted on the client side before upload.

tasks.description: Create a new private S3 bucket and an IAM user with 'PutObject' permissions only.
tasks.success_criteria: Access key and Secret key are generated and stored in a local .env file.

tasks.description: Write a Python function using the 'shutil' library to compress a target folder into a .zip archive.
tasks.success_criteria: A valid .zip file appears in the temp directory after running the script.

tasks.description: Use the 'cryptography' library to encrypt the .zip file using a local secret key.
tasks.success_criteria: The resulting file is unreadable without the secret key.

tasks.description: Use Boto3 to upload the encrypted archive to the specified S3 bucket with a 'yyyy-mm-dd_backup.zip' name.
tasks.success_criteria: The AWS Console shows a new object in the bucket after execution.

tasks.description: Add logic to delete the local temporary .zip files and log the success/failure to a local 'backup.log' file.
tasks.success_criteria: The local temp folder is empty and the log file shows a 'Success' timestamp.

goal: Pass the Cisco Certified Network Associate (CCNA) Exam
description: A structured daily study routine to cover the 200-301 exam topics, including IP connectivity, security fundamentals, and automation.

baseline.fixed_constraints: 90 minutes available every weekday evening.
baseline.physical_constraints: Eye strain from reading PDFs; must use printed books or e-ink where possible.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use Packet Tracer for all lab exercises. No skipping the 'Subnetting' chapter.

tasks.description: Read one sub-chapter of the Official Cert Guide (OCG) covering a specific topic like OSPF or STP.
tasks.success_criteria: The 'Key Topics' section of the chapter is highlighted and notes are taken.
tasks.fallback_version: Watch a 15-minute video summary of the topic.

tasks.description: Open Packet Tracer and configure the topology relevant to the day's reading (e.g., 'Configure Router-on-a-Stick').
tasks.success_criteria: Pings are successful across the configured network segments.
tasks.fallback_version: Open a pre-configured lab and verify the settings using 'show' commands.

tasks.description: Review the Anki deck for command syntax and port numbers.
tasks.success_criteria: All due cards are reviewed to zero.
tasks.fallback_version: Review only the 'Command Line Interface' tag deck.

tasks.description: Take a 10-question practice quiz on the specific domain studied.
tasks.success_criteria: Score at least 80% on the practice set.
tasks.fallback_version: Review the answers of a previous quiz to understand mistakes.

goal: Analyze Network Traffic with Wireshark
description: Capture and dissect a specific protocol exchange (DNS and HTTP) to understand the packet structure and flow.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must run Wireshark with appropriate permissions.
baseline.physical_constraints: Analyzing hex dumps is mentally taxing.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must identify the 3-way handshake explicitly.

tasks.description: Start a Wireshark capture on the main interface and visit a non-HTTPS website (e.g., example.com).
tasks.success_criteria: Capture file saved with at least 500 packets.

tasks.description: Apply a display filter to isolate traffic to the target IP address.
tasks.success_criteria: The packet list shows only relevant conversation packets.

tasks.description: Locate the SYN, SYN-ACK, and ACK packets establishing the connection.
tasks.success_criteria: The relative sequence numbers for the handshake are noted.

tasks.description: Expand the details pane to inspect the Ethernet, IP, and TCP headers for a single data packet.
tasks.success_criteria: The source/dest MAC, IP, and Ports are documented.

tasks.description: Remove the IP filter and search for the DNS 'A' record query that preceded the HTTP connection.
tasks.success_criteria: The DNS Query and Response packets are located.

goal: Master IP Subnetting Speed
description: Achieve the ability to calculate network ID, broadcast IP, and host range for any CIDR block in under 30 seconds.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Practice during commute or idle time.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: No calculators allowed. Mental math or scratchpad only.

tasks.description: Use a subnetting practice app or website to generate 5 random CIDR questions (e.g., /26, /19).
tasks.success_criteria: 5 problems are ready to solve.
tasks.fallback_version: Use a static list of 3 common subnet masks (/24, /30, /28).

tasks.description: Convert the last octet of the IP address and the Subnet Mask into binary for one difficult problem.
tasks.success_criteria: The ANDing operation is visibly written out to find the Network ID.
tasks.fallback_version: Skip binary conversion and use the 'Magic Number' method.

tasks.description: Calculate the first usable host, last usable host, and broadcast address for all 5 problems.
tasks.success_criteria: Answers are written down before checking the solution.
tasks.fallback_version: Solve only for the Network ID.

tasks.description: Check answers against the tool and analyze any off-by-one errors.
tasks.success_criteria: Understanding of why an error occurred is reached.
tasks.fallback_version: Re-read the 'Magic Number' cheat sheet.

goal: Configure a VLAN-Segmented Home Network
description: Isolate IoT devices from the main PC network using a managed switch and a router supporting 802.1Q VLAN tagging.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Hardware: Ubiquiti or generic managed switch.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires crawling under desk to recable devices.
baseline.non_negotiables: Main PC must retain internet access at all times.

tasks.description: Create a table defining VLAN IDs (e.g., 10 for Trusted, 20 for IoT) and their corresponding subnets.
tasks.success_criteria: Schema table is written down (e.g., VLAN 20 = 192.168.20.0/24).

tasks.description: Log into the router and create virtual interfaces for each VLAN with the gateway IP.
tasks.success_criteria: Router shows active status for new virtual interfaces.

tasks.description: Set the uplink port on the managed switch to 'Trunk' or 'All' mode to carry tagged traffic.
tasks.success_criteria: Link light remains active and router is reachable.

tasks.description: Set specific ports on the switch to 'Access' mode for the designated VLAN ID (e.g., Port 4 -> VLAN 20).
tasks.success_criteria: Device plugged into Port 4 receives an IP in the 192.168.20.x range.

tasks.description: Add rules to the router to block traffic from VLAN 20 (IoT) to VLAN 10 (Trusted).
tasks.success_criteria: Ping from IoT device to Main PC fails; Ping to 8.8.8.8 succeeds.

goal: Design and Deploy a Zero Trust Network Architecture for a Small Office
description: Transition a flat office network to a Zero Trust model where no device is trusted by default, utilizing identity-based access and micro-segmentation.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must utilize existing hardware (managed switches and a pfSense router).
baseline.physical_constraints: Implementation must occur after hours (6 PM - 10 PM) to avoid business disruption.
baseline.non_negotiables: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) must be required for all VPN access.

tasks.description: Map all network devices and document the necessary data flows between departments (e.g., HR to Payroll server).
tasks.success_criteria: A complete traffic flow matrix is created and verified against active connections.

tasks.description: Configure an OpenID Connect (OIDC) or RADIUS server to act as the single source of truth for user identities.
tasks.success_criteria: The router can successfully authenticate a test user against the IdP.

tasks.description: Create isolated VLANs for different trust levels (Users, Servers, Guest, IoT) and apply strict inter-VLAN firewall rules.
tasks.success_criteria: Devices in the Guest VLAN cannot ping the Server VLAN.

tasks.task: Set Up WireGuard VPN with MFA
tasks.description: Deploy a WireGuard VPN tunnel for remote access that requires an MFA token for session establishment.
tasks.success_criteria: A remote connection is established only after successful MFA verification.

tasks.description: Configure an 802.1X policy that checks if a connecting device has a valid certificate and up-to-date antivirus.
tasks.success_criteria: A non-compliant laptop is redirected to a remediation VLAN automatically.

goal: Maintain Daily Network Health via Monitoring
description: Sustain 99.9% uptime by proactively reviewing network metrics and logs for anomalies before they lead to outages.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Review performed at the start of the workday (8:30 AM).
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires high focus on time-series graphs.
baseline.non_negotiables: All high-severity alerts must be acknowledged within 30 minutes of the check.

tasks.description: Analyze Grafana or Zabbix dashboards for CPU usage, memory, and interface errors on core routers.
tasks.success_criteria: Verified that all core metrics are within the 'Green' threshold.
tasks.fallback_version: Check only the main ISP gateway bandwidth usage.

tasks.description: Search the last 24 hours of centralized Syslog entries for 'Critical' or 'Emergency' level messages.
tasks.success_criteria: A list of any identified hardware or interface flaps is compiled.
tasks.fallback_version: Scan the top 100 log entries for keywords like 'Error' or 'Failed'.

tasks.description: Run a multi-target ping test to common DNS servers (1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8) to check for jitter or packet loss.
tasks.success_criteria: Average latency is recorded and confirmed to be under 50ms.
tasks.fallback_version: Perform a single fast speed test using the CLI.

tasks.description: Verify that the automated nightly backup of device configurations (e.g., via Oxidized) was successful.
tasks.success_criteria: The 'Last Backup' timestamp for all critical devices is within the last 24 hours.
tasks.fallback_version: Manually trigger a backup for the core router only.

goal: Configure BGP Peering in a Multi-Homed Lab
description: Establish External BGP (eBGP) peering between two Autonomous Systems (AS) to learn about route propagation and path selection.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Using virtual routers (GNS3 or EVE-NG).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the 'AS Path' attribute to influence routing decisions.

tasks.description: Assign private AS numbers and peer IP addresses for the 'Provider' and 'Customer' routers.
tasks.success_criteria: Router interfaces can ping each other directly over the peering link.

tasks.description: Configure the 'neighbor' command on both routers to initiate the eBGP session.
tasks.success_criteria: The command 'show ip bgp summary' shows the neighbor state as 'Established'.

tasks.description: Use the 'network' command to advertise local loopback networks into the BGP table.
tasks.success_criteria: The peer router receives the prefixes and adds them to its routing table.

tasks.description: Configure a route-map to prepend the local AS number multiple times for outgoing advertisements to test path preference.
tasks.success_criteria: The peer router sees the lengthened path and prefers a different route if available.

goal: Weekly Network Protocol Deep-Dive
description: Achieve a master-level understanding of core Internet protocols by reading one RFC (Request for Comments) per week.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Reading sessions occur on Sunday mornings.
baseline.physical_constraints: RFCs are plain text and very long; requires a highlighter tool.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must focus on the 'Header Format' and 'State Machine' sections of each RFC.

tasks.description: Choose a foundational RFC (e.g., RFC 791 for IP, RFC 793 for TCP) from a curated list.
tasks.success_criteria: The PDF or text file is loaded and ready for reading.
tasks.fallback_version: Choose a high-level summary article of the RFC.

tasks.description: Sketch the packet header on a piece of paper, labeling every field and its bit length.
tasks.success_criteria: A complete, accurate header diagram exists for the protocol.
tasks.fallback_version: Print out a pre-made header diagram and highlight the key fields.

tasks.description: Document the transitions between states (e.g., TCP LISTEN to SYN_SENT) based on specific flags.
tasks.success_criteria: A flowchart showing at least 4 state transitions is created.
tasks.fallback_version: Read only the section describing a 'Successful Connection' state.

tasks.description: Write a 3-bullet point list of the protocol's known limitations or security vulnerabilities as described in the RFC.
tasks.success_criteria: The limitations section of the notes is completed.
tasks.fallback_version: Note down just one major vulnerability.

goal: Build an Automated Network Device Inventory Script
description: Create a Python tool that uses Netmiko to SSH into multiple devices and pull serial numbers and hardware models into a spreadsheet.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use Python 3.x and the Netmiko library.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires access to the network management VLAN.
baseline.non_negotiables: Credentials must be prompted at runtime, never stored in the script.

tasks.description: Install Netmiko and create a text file containing the IP addresses of the target devices.
tasks.success_criteria: Python environment is ready and the 'devices.txt' file is populated.

tasks.description: Write the core loop to SSH into a single device and execute a 'show version' command.
tasks.success_criteria: The raw CLI output from one router is printed to the Python console.

tasks.description: Use Regular Expressions (re) to parse the Serial Number and Model Name from the raw command output.
tasks.success_criteria: The script prints only the clean strings: 'Model: C2960X, SN: FOC12345'.

tasks.description: Modify the script to append the extracted data into a CSV file after each device connection.
tasks.success_criteria: A 'inventory.csv' file is generated containing rows for all reachable devices.

tasks.description: Add try-except blocks to catch 'AuthenticationFailed' or 'NetmikoTimeoutException' to prevent script crashes.
tasks.success_criteria: The script continues to the next IP if one device is unreachable.

goal: Build a Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) with Public and Private Subnets
description: Create a secure cloud network environment in AWS or GCP that isolates sensitive database servers from the public internet using a NAT Gateway.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use the cloud provider's web console (no CLI/Terraform yet).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Database subnet must have zero direct inbound routes from the internet.

tasks.description: Create a new VPC with a primary IPv4 CIDR block of 10.0.0.0/16.
tasks.success_criteria: VPC state is marked as 'Available' in the dashboard.

tasks.description: Provision two subnets: one public (10.0.1.0/24) and one private (10.0.2.0/24).
tasks.success_criteria: Two distinct subnet IDs are visible within the new VPC.

tasks.description: Attach an Internet Gateway (IGW) to the VPC and add a route in the public route table (0.0.0.0/0 -> IGW).
tasks.success_criteria: A test instance in the public subnet can successfully ping 8.8.8.8.

tasks.description: Provision a NAT Gateway in the public subnet and update the private route table to route external traffic through it.
tasks.success_criteria: An instance in the private subnet can run 'apt-get update' but cannot be reached via SSH from the internet.

tasks.description: Create a security group for the private subnet that only allows inbound traffic on Port 3306 from the public subnet's IP range.
tasks.success_criteria: The private instance only accepts connections originating from the public instance.

goal: Daily Review of Network Security Vulnerabilities (CVEs)
description: Maintain awareness of emerging threats by reviewing new Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) related to networking hardware and protocols.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during the morning news check.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires high critical thinking to assess relevance to personal lab.
baseline.non_negotiables: Any vulnerability affecting 'Active' hardware in the lab must be logged for patching.

tasks.description: Review the National Vulnerability Database (NVD) or a curated security feed like 'CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities'.
tasks.success_criteria: Scanned the list of entries released in the last 24 hours.
tasks.fallback_version: Read the top 3 headlines on a security news site (e.g., The Hacker News).

tasks.description: Cross-reference the vendors mentioned in high-severity CVEs with the local network inventory (Cisco, Ubiquiti, Synology).
tasks.success_criteria: Determined if any local devices are potentially impacted.
tasks.fallback_version: Only check for vulnerabilities with a CVSS score > 9.0.

tasks.description: Record any relevant CVE IDs in a 'Patch Tracker' and assign a priority level based on exposure.
tasks.success_criteria: A new entry is added to the patch tracker or the session is closed as 'No Action Required'.
tasks.fallback_version: Star the relevant email or bookmark the CVE page.

goal: Set Up a Site-to-Site VPN Between Home and Lab
description: Create a permanent, encrypted tunnel between two geographically separate networks using IPsec to allow seamless resource sharing.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Both sites use static public IPs or dynamic DNS. Both routers must support IPsec.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires simultaneous access to both network consoles.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use AES-256 encryption or higher. Pre-shared keys must be at least 32 characters.

tasks.description: Configure the IKE (Internet Key Exchange) policy on both routers (Encryption: AES-256, Hash: SHA-256, DH Group: 14).
tasks.success_criteria: Router logs show 'Phase 1 successful' or 'IKE_SA established'.

tasks.description: Define the IPsec security association (SA) parameters for data encapsulation.
tasks.success_criteria: Both routers agree on the transform set/proposal.

tasks.task: Define Crypto Map and Traffic Selectors
tasks.description: Specify the local and remote subnets that are allowed to traverse the VPN tunnel.
tasks.success_criteria: The 'Interesting Traffic' ACL is defined on both devices.

tasks.description: Create firewall rules to allow UDP 500/4500 and ESP traffic. Configure NAT Exemption to prevent translation of internal VPN traffic.
tasks.success_criteria: Packets destined for the remote subnet are not being NATed to the public IP.

tasks.description: Perform an ICMP ping from a host in Site A to a host in Site B using internal IP addresses.
tasks.success_criteria: The ping returns successfully and the VPN tunnel status is 'Active/Up'.

goal: Weekly Network Documentation Update
description: Maintain a 'living' network diagram and IP address management (IPAM) sheet to prevent IP conflicts and improve disaster recovery.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done on Friday afternoons. Using Lucidchart or Visio.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: All changes made during the week must be reflected in the 'Current' version.

tasks.description: Review the DHCP server lease table for any unexpected devices or new static assignments.
tasks.success_criteria: The IPAM spreadsheet is updated with any new reserved IPs.
tasks.fallback_version: Verify only the 'Static' reservation list.

tasks.description: Verify if any cables were moved or new switches added and update the physical topology diagram.
tasks.success_criteria: Diagram version date is updated and reflects the current port usage.
tasks.fallback_version: Note changes on a sticky note for a bi-weekly update.

tasks.description: Check if any administrative passwords or SSH keys are due for rotation according to the 90-day policy.
tasks.success_criteria: A task is created for any keys requiring immediate rotation.
tasks.fallback_version: Check only the core router admin password.

goal: Build a Simple Software-Defined Network (SDN) Controller
description: Develop a Python script that uses the Northbound API of a controller (like Ryu or Floodlight) to dynamically push flow entries to OpenvSwitch.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Using Mininet for network emulation. Python 3.x.
baseline.physical_constraints: SDN logic is abstract; requires a whiteboard to visualize flow tables.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to block a specific MAC address via an API call.

tasks.description: Launch a single switch, three-host topology in Mininet connected to a remote controller.
tasks.success_criteria: Command 'pingall' succeeds and the controller logs see the switch connection.

tasks.description: Write a Python script that uses the 'requests' library to query the controller's REST API for flow table stats.
tasks.success_criteria: The script prints a JSON object containing current flow statistics.

tasks.description: Implement a function that sends a POST request to the controller to add a high-priority 'DROP' flow for a specific IP.
tasks.success_criteria: The script returns a 200/201 status code from the API.

tasks.description: Verify the flow entry in the switch and confirm that the targeted host can no longer communicate.
tasks.success_criteria: Mininet command 'h1 ping h2' fails only after the script is executed.

tasks.description: Add a function to clear the specific flow entry and restore network connectivity.
tasks.success_criteria: Mininet command 'h1 ping h2' succeeds again.

goal: Master OSI Model Layers and Encapsulation Logic
description: Develop a deep understanding of how data moves through the stack by studying one OSI layer per day and tracing the encapsulation process.
