baseline.fixed_constraints: Study sessions occur during the morning commute (30 minutes).
baseline.physical_constraints: Limited to reading or watching videos on a mobile device.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to name the Data Unit (PDU) for each layer without looking.

tasks.description: Read the technical specifications and primary functions of the designated 'Layer of the Day' (e.g., Layer 3 - Network).
tasks.success_criteria: Completion of the reading section for that specific layer.
tasks.fallback_version: Watch a 5-minute 'OSI Layer Summary' video for the current layer.

tasks.description: Identify and write down the Protocol Data Unit (e.g., Segment, Packet, Frame) associated with the current layer.
tasks.success_criteria: Correct PDU is recorded in the study log.
tasks.fallback_version: Repeat the PDU name out loud five times.

tasks.description: Draw or mentally map how a piece of data is wrapped with headers from the Application layer down to the current layer.
tasks.success_criteria: Successful mental or physical trace of at least 3 layers of encapsulation.
tasks.fallback_version: View a diagram of the OSI encapsulation process.

goal: Deploy a Redundant Load Balancer with HAProxy and Keepalived
description: Configure a high-availability entry point for a web cluster that automatically fails over to a backup node if the primary node goes down.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use two Ubuntu Linux virtual machines.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires stable local network connectivity for heartbeat signals.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use a Virtual IP (VIP) that floats between nodes.

tasks.description: Install the HAProxy package and verify that the service starts correctly on both virtual machines.
tasks.success_criteria: Running 'haproxy -v' returns a version number on both machines.

tasks.description: Update haproxy.cfg to point to dummy backend servers and implement basic HTTP health checks.
tasks.success_criteria: HAProxy logs show backend servers transitioning from 'DOWN' to 'UP' when active.

tasks.description: Install Keepalived and configure the VRRP instance to define the 'MASTER' and 'BACKUP' roles.
tasks.success_criteria: The Virtual IP is successfully assigned to the 'MASTER' node's interface.

tasks.description: Stop the HAProxy service on the MASTER node and observe if the VIP moves to the BACKUP node.
tasks.success_criteria: The VIP is reachable via ping during and after the primary node shutdown.

tasks.description: Record the final working configuration and VIP addresses in the infrastructure log.
tasks.success_criteria: A document exists with the node IPs, VIP, and failover timing results.

goal: Weekly Network Traffic Profiling with Ntopng
description: Maintain a secure home network by performing a weekly analysis of top talkers, application usage, and potential data leaks.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Analysis performed on Saturday mornings.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires access to the local monitoring dashboard.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must investigate any traffic to unknown foreign IP addresses.

tasks.description: Identify the top 5 devices by bandwidth usage in the last 7 days and verify they are authorized.
tasks.success_criteria: A list of the 5 highest-traffic MAC addresses is validated.
tasks.fallback_version: Check only the total bandwidth usage of the main gateway.

tasks.description: Review the percentage of traffic dedicated to specific protocols (e.g., HTTPS, BitTorrent, DNS).
tasks.success_criteria: Confirmed that no unexpected high-bandwidth protocols are active.
tasks.fallback_version: Look at the 'Alerts' tab in the monitoring tool for 5 minutes.

tasks.description: Analyze the map of destination countries for outgoing traffic and flag any unexpected regions.
tasks.success_criteria: All high-traffic destination countries are accounted for by known services.
tasks.fallback_version: Check only the list of 'External IP' destinations for the primary server.

goal: Build a Custom Network Speed-Test CLI in Python
description: Create a tool that measures upload/download speeds and latency, logging results to a file for long-term ISP performance tracking.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use the 'speedtest-cli' library as a backend.
baseline.physical_constraints: Test results may be inaccurate if other devices are streaming on the network.
baseline.non_negotiables: Results must be exported to a CSV file with a timestamp.

tasks.description: Set up a virtual environment and install the speedtest-cli and pandas libraries.
tasks.success_criteria: Pip list shows the required libraries are installed.

tasks.description: Write a function to initialize the Speedtest object, find the best server, and run download/upload tests.
tasks.success_criteria: The script prints a numeric value for download speed (in Mbps) to the console.

tasks.description: Format the test results into a dictionary with a timestamp and append it to a CSV file.
tasks.success_criteria: A file 'speed_log.csv' is created with headers and one row of data.

tasks.description: Add logic to handle network timeouts or 'No Server Found' errors gracefully.
tasks.success_criteria: The script logs a 'Connection Error' instead of crashing when internet is disconnected.

goal: Daily Practice of IPv6 Addressing and Notation
description: Gain fluency in IPv6 by practicing address compression, expansion, and identifying address types (Link-Local, Global Unicast).

baseline.fixed_constraints: Completed during morning coffee.
baseline.physical_constraints: Long hex strings require careful transcription.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the double-colon rule correctly for zero compression.

tasks.description: Take 3 fully-expanded IPv6 addresses and apply the zero-omission and double-colon rules to compress them.
tasks.success_criteria: All 3 addresses are correctly compressed according to RFC standards.
tasks.fallback_version: Compress only 1 address instead of 3.

tasks.description: Identify if a given IPv6 address is a Global Unicast, Link-Local (fe80::), or Unique Local (fc00::).
tasks.success_criteria: Correct classification of at least 5 addresses.
tasks.fallback_version: Identify only the Link-Local address among a list.

tasks.description: Write out the first 16 bits in binary for a Global Unicast address to understand the prefix range.
tasks.success_criteria: The binary representation matches the hexadecimal prefix.
tasks.fallback_version: Look at an IPv6 prefix cheat sheet for 2 minutes.

goal: Master Wireless LAN (WLAN) Configuration and Security
description: Establish a consistent routine for studying 802.11 standards, frame types, and WPA3 security protocols to prepare for wireless certification.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Limited to 1 hour after work on weekdays.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use a dedicated wireless access point for testing (no messing with the home production Wi-Fi).

tasks.description: Study the modulation, frequency, and maximum speeds of a specific standard (e.g., 802.11ax vs. 802.11ac).
tasks.success_criteria: Completion of one technical chapter and a 3-sentence summary of the standard's advantages.
tasks.fallback_version: Watch a 5-minute video on the history of Wi-Fi standards.

tasks.description: Use a wireless adapter in monitor mode to capture 802.11 management frames (Beacons, Probes) in Wireshark.
tasks.success_criteria: Identification of at least one Beacon frame and its SSID in the packet details.
tasks.fallback_version: Review a pcap file of wireless traffic from an online repository.

tasks.description: Analyze the 4-way WPA2/WPA3 handshake in a packet capture to understand key exchange.
tasks.success_criteria: The 'Message 1 of 4' and 'Message 2 of 4' packets are located and inspected.
tasks.fallback_version: Read a blog post explaining the 4-way handshake process.

goal: Deploy an OpenVPN Server on a Raspberry Pi
description: Set up a private VPN server to allow secure remote access to the home network from public hotspots.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Raspberry Pi 4 with Raspberry Pi OS installed.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires physical access to the router for port forwarding.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use certificate-based authentication (no shared secrets only).

tasks.description: Execute the automated PiVPN installation script and select OpenVPN as the protocol.
tasks.success_criteria: The installation wizard completes without errors and requests a reboot.

tasks.description: Log into the home router and forward UDP port 1194 to the Raspberry Pi's static local IP.
tasks.success_criteria: An external port scanner shows the port as 'filtered' or 'open' depending on the ISP.

tasks.description: Create a new .ovpn profile for a mobile device and export it via secure copy (SCP).
tasks.success_criteria: The .ovpn file is successfully transferred to the target mobile device.

tasks.description: Disconnect the mobile device from Wi-Fi, enable cellular data, and initiate the VPN connection.
tasks.success_criteria: The device connects successfully and can access the internal IP of the Raspberry Pi.

goal: Weekly Linux Network Troubleshooting Drills
description: Practice diagnosing common connectivity issues using standard Linux CLI tools (ip, nmcli, ss, traceroute).

baseline.fixed_constraints: Saturday morning sessions (45 minutes).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must document the exact command used to find the 'root cause'.

tasks.description: Use the 'ip addr' and 'ip link' commands to verify interface status, MTU, and IP assignments.
tasks.success_criteria: A table of current interface states is created in the lab log.
tasks.fallback_version: Use 'ifconfig' (deprecated) just to see basic stats.

tasks.description: Analyze the output of 'ip route' to determine the default gateway and any specific static routes.
tasks.success_criteria: The current default gateway IP is identified correctly.
tasks.fallback_version: Run 'route -n' to see the legacy routing table format.

tasks.description: Use the 'ss -tulpn' command to identify all listening ports and the processes associated with them.
tasks.success_criteria: A list of all active TCP/UDP listeners and their PIDs is generated.
tasks.fallback_version: Use 'netstat' for a similar but slower output.

tasks.description: Perform a 'mtr' (My Traceroute) to a public target and analyze each hop for packet loss.
tasks.success_criteria: The hop with the highest jitter or loss is identified.
tasks.fallback_version: Run a standard 'traceroute' command.

goal: Implement a Network-Wide Ad Blocker (Pi-hole)
description: Deploy a DNS-based ad blocker to protect all devices on the network from tracking and advertisements.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Requires a Raspberry Pi or a Linux VM.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires administrative access to the router's DNS settings.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must not break DNS resolution for critical work devices.

tasks.description: Run the official Pi-hole installation script and configure a static IP for the server.
tasks.success_criteria: The web admin interface (pi.hole/admin) is accessible from a browser.

tasks.description: Select a secure upstream provider (e.g., Unbound or Cloudflare) within the Pi-hole settings.
tasks.success_criteria: Pi-hole successfully resolves a test domain (e.g., google.com).

tasks.description: Change the LAN DNS settings on the home router to point to the Pi-hole server's IP.
tasks.success_criteria: Devices on the network begin appearing in the Pi-hole query log.

tasks.description: Add one additional 'Adlist' from a community-verified source and trigger a gravity update.
tasks.success_criteria: The total number of blocked domains in the dashboard increases.

goal: Daily Practice of Wireshark Display Filter Syntax
description: Master the syntax for complex display filters to quickly find specific traffic in large capture files.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done at the start of the study session (10 mins).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use logical operators (&&, ||, !) in every practice session.

tasks.description: Write a filter to find all HTTP POST requests or all DNS responses with a specific error code.
tasks.success_criteria: The filter turns 'green' (valid syntax) and correctly isolates the target packets.
tasks.fallback_version: Use the 'Expression Builder' GUI to create the filter.

tasks.description: Combine two filters (e.g., 'ip.addr == X and tcp.port == Y') to narrow down a specific conversation.
tasks.success_criteria: The resulting packet list contains only the specified intersection of traffic.
tasks.fallback_version: Write only a single-parameter filter.

goal: Master Big O Notation and Complexity Analysis
description: Develop the ability to instinctively identify the Time and Space complexity of any given code snippet through daily practice.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Practice during lunch break (20 mins).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must differentiate between Average Case and Worst Case scenarios.

tasks.description: Select a random function from a 'Cracking the Coding Interview' solution repository.
tasks.success_criteria: A snippet with at least one loop or recursive call is visible.
tasks.fallback_version: Use a simple nested loop example from a tutorial site.

tasks.description: Identify the number of iterations relative to input 'N' and note any nested operations.
tasks.success_criteria: The relationship (linear, quadratic, logarithmic) is written down.
tasks.fallback_version: Trace the loop for N=5 to see the growth.

tasks.description: If the function is recursive, draw the first 3 levels of the recursion tree to determine the branching factor.
tasks.success_criteria: The depth and width of the tree are estimated.
tasks.fallback_version: Skip if the snippet is iterative.

tasks.description: Write down the final Big O notation for Time and Space (e.g., O(N log N)).
tasks.success_criteria: Answer is compared against the provided solution.
tasks.fallback_version: Guess based on the 'common complexities' cheat sheet.

tasks.description: Determine if specific inputs (e.g., sorted array) change the complexity (Best Case vs. Worst Case).
tasks.success_criteria: One edge case scenario is noted.
tasks.fallback_version: Assume average case only.

goal: Implement a Resizable Dynamic Array (Vector) from Scratch
description: Build a custom `ArrayList` class in C++ or Java to understand memory allocation, resizing logic, and amortized analysis.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use raw arrays/pointers, not built-in vector libraries.
baseline.physical_constraints: Debugging memory leaks in C++ can be tedious.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must implement the geometric resizing strategy (doubling capacity).

tasks.description: Create a class with a private array pointer, `size` (current elements), and `capacity` (total slots).
tasks.success_criteria: Class compiles and constructor initializes an empty array of capacity 2.

tasks.description: Write the method to add an element to the end of the array if `size < capacity`.
tasks.success_criteria: Adding 2 elements works; adding a 3rd (without resize logic) would fail or segfault.

tasks.description: Write a private `resize()` method that creates a new array of double the capacity, copies elements, and deletes the old array.
tasks.success_criteria: Pushing the 3rd element triggers a resize, updating capacity to 4.

tasks.description: Add methods to access or modify elements at a specific index, throwing an error if out of bounds.
tasks.success_criteria: Accessing index -1 or index >= size throws an exception.

tasks.description: Write a short comment block explaining why appending N elements takes O(N) time total, not O(N^2).
tasks.success_criteria: The explanation correctly cites the geometric series sum.

goal: Build an LRU (Least Recently Used) Cache
description: Combine a Hash Map and a Doubly Linked List to create an O(1) access cache with eviction policies.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must pass the LeetCode #146 test suite or local unit tests.
baseline.physical_constraints: Pointer manipulation in Doubly Linked Lists is error-prone.
baseline.non_negotiables: Get and Put operations must both be O(1).

tasks.description: Create a Doubly Linked List Node class containing `key`, `value`, `prev`, and `next` pointers.
tasks.success_criteria: Node class is defined and can be instantiated.

tasks.description: Write helper functions to add a node right after the 'head' dummy and remove a node from anywhere in the list.
tasks.success_criteria: Unit tests confirm nodes can be inserted and deleted without breaking the chain.

tasks.description: Check the HashMap for the key. If found, move the corresponding node to the front (head) and return value.
tasks.success_criteria: Accessing an item updates its position in the linked list to the front.

tasks.description: Add new key-value pairs. If capacity is exceeded, remove the LRU node (tail's previous) and delete from Map.
tasks.success_criteria: Adding beyond capacity removes the least recently used item correctly.

tasks.description: Execute a sequence of Get/Put operations and verify the state of the cache matches expected results.
tasks.success_criteria: All assertions pass.

goal: Daily Graph Traversal Visualization (BFS/DFS)
description: Develop intuition for graph algorithms by manually tracing Breadth-First and Depth-First searches on random graphs.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done on a whiteboard or tablet.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must track the 'Queue' (for BFS) or 'Stack' (for DFS) state explicitly.

tasks.description: Sketch a graph with 6-8 nodes and 10 edges, including at least one cycle.
tasks.success_criteria: Graph is clearly drawn with labeled nodes (A, B, C...).
tasks.fallback_version: Use a printed graph problem from a textbook.

tasks.description: Choose either BFS or DFS and a starting node (e.g., Node A).
tasks.success_criteria: Choice is written at the top of the page.
tasks.fallback_version: Always start with Node A.

tasks.description: Step through the algorithm, updating the 'Visited' set and the Queue/Stack data structure on paper for each step.
tasks.success_criteria: The order of visitation (e.g., A -> B -> D...) is recorded.
tasks.fallback_version: Trace only the first 3 steps.

tasks.description: Identify 'Tree Edges' vs 'Back Edges' (for DFS) during the traversal.
tasks.success_criteria: At least one back edge (if a cycle exists) is identified.
tasks.fallback_version: Skip edge classification.

tasks.description: Run the same graph/algorithm in a visualizer tool (like VisuAlgo) to verify the manual trace.
tasks.success_criteria: Manual trace matches the tool's output.
tasks.fallback_version: Double-check the logic manually.

goal: Implement a Binary Heap (Priority Queue)
description: Construct a Min-Heap using an array-based representation to understand tree-to-array mapping and the 'sift-up/sift-down' operations.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python or Java.
baseline.physical_constraints: Index arithmetic (parent/child calculations) requires care.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must implement both `insert` and `extract_min`.

tasks.description: Write functions to calculate `parent(i)`, `left_child(i)`, and `right_child(i)` for a given index `i`.
tasks.success_criteria: Unit tests confirm correct indices (e.g., parent(1) -> 0).

tasks.description: Write the logic to swap a node with its parent if the heap property is violated (used during insertion).
tasks.success_criteria: A manually constructed violated array is restored to heap property.

tasks.description: Add the new element to the end of the array and call the 'Bubble Up' function.

tasks.description: Write a function to return the difference between the heights of the left and right subtrees.
tasks.success_criteria: Function returns a value in the range [-2, 2].

tasks.description: Insert a node and, while unwinding the recursion, check the balance factor and perform the appropriate rotation (LL, RR, LR, RL).
tasks.success_criteria: The tree remains balanced after inserting the numbers 1 through 10 in sequential order.

goal: Weekly Practice of the Boyer-Moore Voting Algorithm
description: Maintain proficiency in finding the majority element in an array using O(N) time and O(1) space.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Sunday morning logic review.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to explain why the algorithm works for elements appearing more than N/2 times.

tasks.description: Trace a 10-element array on paper, updating the 'candidate' and 'count' variables for each step.
tasks.success_criteria: The correct majority element is identified on paper.
tasks.fallback_version: Watch a 2-minute animation of the Boyer-Moore process.

tasks.description: Implement the majority element finder in a code editor without referring to external sources.
tasks.success_criteria: The function passes a local test case with a clear majority element.
tasks.fallback_version: Write the pseudocode for the 'if count == 0' condition.

goal: Implement KMP (Knuth-Morris-Pratt) String Matching
description: Learn to search for a pattern in a text in O(N+M) time by avoiding redundant comparisons using a prefix function (LPS array).

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python or Java.
baseline.physical_constraints: The LPS (Longest Proper Prefix which is also Suffix) calculation is counter-intuitive.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must not use any built-in 'substring' or 'find' methods.

tasks.description: Write the function to pre-process the pattern and build the Longest Proper Prefix array.
tasks.success_criteria: For pattern 'ababc', the LPS array is [0, 0, 1, 2, 0].

tasks.description: Use the LPS array to skip characters in the text when a mismatch occurs during pattern matching.
tasks.success_criteria: The script correctly identifies the starting index of 'ababc' in 'ababdababc'.

tasks.description: Count the total number of character comparisons made by KMP vs a Brute Force approach on a highly repetitive string.
tasks.success_criteria: A written note shows that KMP performed significantly fewer comparisons.

goal: Build an External Merge Sort for Large Datasets
description: Implement a sorting algorithm that can handle datasets larger than the available RAM by using temporary disk storage and a k-way merge.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Limited to 128MB of RAM for the sorting process. Dataset size is 1GB.
baseline.physical_constraints: Disk I/O is the primary bottleneck; require efficient buffering.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the standard filesystem for temporary 'runs'. No external database allowed.

tasks.description: Read the 1GB file in 100MB chunks, sort each chunk in memory, and save it as a sorted 'run' file on disk.
tasks.success_criteria: Ten individual files exist on disk, each containing sorted integers.

tasks.description: Open all 10 sorted runs and read the first 10MB of each into memory buffers.
tasks.success_criteria: Program maintains 10 active file pointers and buffers without exceeding the 128MB RAM limit.

tasks.description: Use a Min-Heap to compare the top elements of each buffer, writing the smallest to the final output file and replenishing the buffer as needed.
tasks.success_criteria: A single 1GB output file is created where every line is in ascending order.

goal: Daily Practice of Dynamic Programming: State Space Reduction
description: Master the technique of reducing the space complexity of DP solutions (e.g., from O(N^2) to O(N)) by identifying necessary states.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Evening study session (30 mins).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must write both the full 2D array logic and the optimized 1D/variable logic for comparison.

tasks.description: Select a 2D DP problem (e.g., Longest Common Subsequence) and write the recurrence: dp[i][j] = f(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).
tasks.success_criteria: Correct dependency on the 'previous row' or 'previous column' is identified.
tasks.fallback_version: Read the 'Space Optimization' section of a solved problem on LeetCode.

tasks.description: Implement the solution using only two rows (or one row) if the current state only depends on the previous row.
tasks.success_criteria: Code passes the same test cases as the 2D version but uses significantly less memory.
tasks.fallback_version: Draw a diagram showing how a 1D array can be updated in-place.

goal: Implement a Fenwick Tree (Binary Indexed Tree) for Prefix Sums
description: Build a Fenwick Tree to efficiently calculate prefix sums and perform point updates in O(log N) time.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Java or Python.
baseline.physical_constraints: The 'i & -i' bitwise logic is hard to visualize; requires a bitwise truth table.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must support 1-based indexing for the internal array logic.

tasks.description: Verify the behavior of the `LSB(i) = i & -i` function for indices 1 through 16.
tasks.success_criteria: A table is created showing the Lowbit value for the first 16 integers.

tasks.description: Write the `update(i, delta)` function that traverses 'up' the tree by adding LSB(i) to the current index.
tasks.success_criteria: The tree structure is correctly updated when a value is added to a specific index.

tasks.description: Write the `query(i)` function that traverses 'down' the tree by subtracting LSB(i) to aggregate the prefix sum.
tasks.success_criteria: Querying index 5 returns the sum of elements from 1 to 5 correctly.

goal: Weekly Advanced Graph Algorithm: Tarjan's for SCC
description: Learn and practice finding Strongly Connected Components (SCC) in directed graphs using Tarjan's algorithm.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Saturday afternoon deep-dive (1 hour).
baseline.physical_constraints: Tracking 'low-link' values and 'discovery times' is mentally taxing.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use a stack to track nodes currently in the recursion tail.

tasks.description: Draw a directed graph with 6 nodes and 2 cycles, then manually calculate the low-link value for each node.
tasks.success_criteria: Each node is labeled with a discovery time and a correct low-link value.
tasks.fallback_version: Watch a 10-minute visual walkthrough of Tarjan's algorithm.

tasks.description: Implement the DFS-based Tarjan's algorithm to identify all SCCs in a given adjacency list.
tasks.success_criteria: The function returns a list of sets, where each set is an SCC (e.g., [{A, B}, {C, D, E}, {F}]).
tasks.fallback_version: Fill in the blanks of a provided Tarjan's algorithm template.

goal: Build a Concurrent Rate Limiter using the Token Bucket Algorithm
description: Implement a thread-safe rate limiter that allows a burst of requests up to a capacity and then restricts to a constant rate.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Java (with AtomicLong/Locks) or Go (with channels/mutexes).
baseline.physical_constraints: Timing precision may vary based on OS scheduling.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to handle 1000 requests per second across 4 threads.

tasks.description: Write logic to calculate how many tokens should be added to the bucket based on the time elapsed since the last request.
tasks.success_criteria: The token count correctly increases at the defined rate without exceeding the capacity.

tasks.description: Write a thread-safe function that checks for available tokens, decrements the count if possible, and returns a boolean.
tasks.success_criteria: A single-threaded test shows the bucket empties after 'capacity' requests and then refills.

tasks.description: Spawn multiple threads attempting to consume tokens simultaneously and verify no race conditions occur.
tasks.success_criteria: The total number of 'allowed' requests over 10 seconds matches the (initial capacity + rate * time) within 1% error.

goal: Build a Topological Sort Tool for Task Scheduling
description: Implement Kahn's algorithm using an in-degree array and a queue to find a valid linear ordering of tasks with dependencies.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python 3.x.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires visualizing the graph to verify the output order.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must detect if the graph contains a cycle (rendering scheduling impossible).

tasks.task: Build Adjacency List and In-Degree Array
tasks.description: Process a list of pairs (u, v) representing 'u must be done before v' to create a graph and a count of incoming edges for each node.
tasks.success_criteria: The in-degree of a node with 2 prerequisites is correctly identified as 2.

tasks.description: Identify all nodes with an in-degree of 0 and add them to a FIFO queue.
tasks.success_criteria: The queue contains all tasks that have no prerequisites.

tasks.description: While the queue is not empty, remove a node, add it to the result, and decrement the in-degree of its neighbors. Add neighbors to the queue if their in-degree becomes 0.
tasks.success_criteria: The final list contains all nodes in an order where every prerequisite appears before its dependent task.

tasks.description: Compare the number of nodes in the sorted result with the total number of nodes in the graph.
tasks.success_criteria: The function returns an error or empty list if a cycle exists (result count < total nodes).

goal: Daily Practice of Binary Search Tree (BST) Validation
description: Master the 'Range Bound' technique for validating a BST to avoid the common mistake of only checking immediate children.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during morning commute (15 mins).
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires mental focus on recursive call stacks.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the (min, max) constraint passing method.

tasks.description: Draw a 3-level tree and note the (min, max) allowed range for each node (e.g., right child of root 10 has range [11, inf]).
tasks.success_criteria: Each node in the sketch is correctly labeled with its valid numerical bounds.
tasks.fallback_version: Read a blog post on why 'left < root < right' check is insufficient.

tasks.description: Write a `isValidBST(node, min, max)` function that returns false if `node.val` falls outside the range.
tasks.success_criteria: The function correctly identifies a sub-tree violation (e.g., 20 is the right child of 10, but is in the left sub-tree of 15).
tasks.fallback_version: Write the iterative in-order traversal version instead.

goal: Implement a Bloom Filter for Probabilistic Set Membership
description: Create a space-efficient data structure that can tell if an element is 'definitely not in a set' or 'possibly in a set' using multiple hash functions.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python with a bit-array library (or integer bitwise ops).
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires understanding the trade-off between bit-array size and false positive rate.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use at least 3 different seed-based hash functions.

tasks.description: Initialize an integer of length M to act as a bit-array and define 3 hash functions (e.g., using different seeds with `mmh3` or `hashlib`).
tasks.success_criteria: An item produces 3 distinct indices within the range of M.

tasks.description: For a given input string, compute the 3 hash indices and set those bits to 1 in the bit-array.
tasks.success_criteria: The bit-array has exactly the number of '1's expected for the number of inserted items (accounting for collisions).

tasks.description: For a query string, verify if all 3 bit indices are set to 1. Return false if any bit is 0.
tasks.success_criteria: The function never returns 'false' for an item that was definitely added.

tasks.description: Add 100 items, then check 1000 random strings not in the set to calculate the empirical false positive rate.
tasks.success_criteria: The calculated rate matches the theoretical formula `(1 - e^(-kn/m))^k`.

goal: Weekly Dynamic Programming: Bitmask Deep-Dive
description: Learn to solve NP-hard problems on small constraints (N < 20) by using bitmasks to represent subsets in DP states.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Saturday morning session (1.5 hours).
baseline.physical_constraints: Binary state representation is cognitively heavy.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must solve at least one variation of the Traveling Salesperson Problem (TSP).

tasks.description: Write down how an integer (e.g., 13 which is 1101 in binary) represents having visited cities 0, 2, and 3.
tasks.success_criteria: Correct mapping of bit indices to set elements is verified.
tasks.fallback_version: Read a tutorial on 'DP with Bitmasking' for 20 minutes.

tasks.description: Write the logic to check if a bit is set `(mask >> i) & 1` and to set a bit `mask | (1 << i)`.
tasks.success_criteria: Helper functions for bit checking and setting are functional.
tasks.fallback_version: Use the Python `bin()` function to debug bit values.

tasks.description: Implement the recursive DP with memoization for a 4-city TSP problem using `dp(mask, current_city)`.
tasks.success_criteria: The algorithm finds the shortest Hamiltonian path for a small adjacency matrix.
tasks.fallback_version: Trace the DP table for a 3-city problem on paper.

goal: Implement Huffman Coding for Data Compression
description: Build a lossless data compression tool by constructing an optimal prefix-free binary tree based on character frequencies.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python with a Min-Heap (heapq).
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires precise tree traversal to generate variable-length codes.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must produce the mapping of characters to binary strings (e.g., 'a' -> 110).

tasks.description: Write a function to count the occurrences of each character in an input string.
tasks.success_criteria: Input 'banana' returns {'b': 1, 'a': 3, 'n': 2}.

tasks.description: Use a Min-Heap to repeatedly merge the two smallest frequency nodes into a parent node until only one root remains.
tasks.success_criteria: The root node's frequency equals the total length of the input string.

tasks.description: Traverse the Huffman tree recursively, assigning '0' for left branches and '1' for right branches to create a lookup table.
tasks.success_criteria: No code is a prefix of another code (e.g., if 'a' is 0, 'b' cannot be 01).

tasks.description: Encode the original string into a binary string and then successfully decode it back using the Huffman tree.
tasks.success_criteria: The decoded string is identical to the original input.

goal: Master SQL Query Optimization via EXPLAIN ANALYZE
description: Develop the ability to read execution plans and optimize slow queries by analyzing one complex query daily.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Practice on a local PostgreSQL instance with the 'Pagila' sample dataset.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must reduce the query cost by at least 20% or explain why it is already optimal.

tasks.description: Write a query involving at least 2 JOINs and an aggregate function, then run `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` to record the initial execution time.
tasks.success_criteria: Initial query cost and execution time are logged.
tasks.fallback_version: Use a pre-written slow query from a tutorial.

tasks.description: Identify the most expensive node in the execution plan (e.g., Sequential Scan, Nested Loop) and hypothesize a fix.
tasks.success_criteria: The bottleneck node is correctly identified.
tasks.fallback_version: Paste the plan into a visualizer tool (e.g., depesz) to find the red nodes.

tasks.description: Create a specific B-Tree or Hash index on the columns used in the WHERE or JOIN clauses.
tasks.success_criteria: The index is created successfully.
tasks.fallback_version: Rewrite the query to avoid a specific expensive operation (e.g., removing a wildcard).

tasks.description: Re-run `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` to verify that the optimizer is using the new index and that costs have decreased.
tasks.success_criteria: The new execution plan shows an 'Index Scan' instead of 'Seq Scan'.
tasks.fallback_version: Drop the index and try a composite index instead.

goal: Design a Cassandra Schema for a Chat Application
description: Create a denormalized data model optimized for write-heavy chat workloads, focusing on Partition Keys and Clustering Columns.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use a free tier of Datastax Astra or local Cassandra container.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires shifting mindset from 'Relational' to 'Query-First' design.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must support the query: 'Get all messages for Room X ordered by time'.

tasks.description: List the top 3 queries the application needs to support (e.g., 'View Room', 'User Login', 'Search Message').
tasks.success_criteria: Queries are written down in plain English.

tasks.description: Design the `messages` table, selecting `room_id` as the Partition Key and `timestamp` as the Clustering Column.
tasks.success_criteria: The `CREATE TABLE` statement includes the correct `PRIMARY KEY ((room_id), timestamp)` syntax.

tasks.description: Design a second table `messages_by_user` to support the 'Get messages by specific user' query, accepting data duplication.
tasks.success_criteria: A second table schema is defined to satisfy the secondary query pattern.

tasks.description: Insert 1000 sample messages and run the main 'View Room' query to verify sorting order.
tasks.success_criteria: Query returns messages in descending timestamp order.

goal: Implement Application-Level Database Sharding
description: Build a Python wrapper that routes user data to different SQLite database files based on a hash of the User ID.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python `sqlite3`.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use a deterministic hash function (e.g., `UserID % 2`).

tasks.description: Create two empty SQLite databases: `shard_0.db` and `shard_1.db` with identical `users` tables.
tasks.success_criteria: Both files exist and contain the correct schema.

tasks.description: Write a function `get_db_connection(user_id)` that returns a connection to shard 0 or 1 based on modulo arithmetic.
tasks.success_criteria: User ID 2 connects to shard 0; User ID 3 connects to shard 1.

tasks.description: Create a script to insert 50 random users using the router function.
tasks.success_criteria: Data is distributed roughly evenly across both database files.

tasks.description: Write a function `get_all_users()` that queries both shards and combines the results into a single list.
tasks.success_criteria: The function returns all 50 users from both files.

goal: Demonstrate Database Transaction Isolation Anomalies
description: Write scripts to intentionally reproduce 'Dirty Reads' and 'Non-Repeatable Reads' to understand ACID compliance.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use MySQL or PostgreSQL (SQLite has limited isolation support).
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires running two simultaneous terminal sessions.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must explicitly set the transaction isolation level to 'READ UNCOMMITTED' for the Dirty Read test.

tasks.description: Create a `accounts` table with a single row: `balance = 100`.
tasks.success_criteria: Table exists and is populated.

tasks.description: Open Transaction A and update balance to 200 (no commit). Open Transaction B (Read Uncommitted) and read the balance.
tasks.success_criteria: Transaction B reads 200 before Transaction A commits.

tasks.description: In Transaction A (Read Committed), read balance. In B, update balance to 300 and commit. In A, read balance again.
tasks.success_criteria: Transaction A sees two different values for the same row within the same transaction.

tasks.description: Repeat Task 3 but set Transaction A's isolation level to 'SERIALIZABLE'.
tasks.success_criteria: Transaction A sees the original value consistently, or the second read fails with a serialization error.

goal: Refactor a Spreadsheet into a Normalized Database (3NF)
description: Convert a flat, messy Excel sheet of sales data into a Third Normal Form (3NF) SQL schema to eliminate redundancy.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Input is a single CSV file with mixed customer, product, and order data.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Final schema must have separate tables for Customers, Products, and Orders.

tasks.description: Review the CSV for any cells containing multiple values (e.g., 'Product A, Product B') and plan to split them into atomic rows.
tasks.success_criteria: A cleaned version of the CSV with atomic values is saved.

tasks.description: Identify columns that depend only on part of the primary key and move them to new tables (e.g., move Customer Name out of the Orders table).
tasks.success_criteria: Draft schemas for `Customers` and `Products` tables are written.

tasks.description: Remove transitive dependencies (e.g., if 'Zip Code' determines 'City', move Zip/City to a separate location lookup table).
tasks.success_criteria: Final ERD (Entity Relationship Diagram) shows no non-key dependencies.

tasks.description: Write a script to read the clean CSV and insert data into the new normalized SQL tables using foreign keys.
tasks.success_criteria: SQL queries on the new tables return the same total sales figures as the original spreadsheet.

goal: Build a Real-Time Distributed Key-Value Store
description: Develop a simplified version of a NoSQL database that supports concurrent reads/writes and basic networking using Go and TCP sockets.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must run on a local network. Using Go 1.21+.
baseline.physical_constraints: High cognitive load when debugging race conditions; sessions limited to 90 minutes.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be thread-safe. Must support 'SET', 'GET', and 'DEL' commands.

tasks.description: Create a thread-safe wrapper around a Go map using sync.RWMutex to store string keys and values.
tasks.success_criteria: A unit test confirms concurrent writes do not cause a panic.

tasks.description: Write a parser for a subset of the REdis Serialization Protocol (RESP) to handle incoming command strings.
tasks.success_criteria: Parser correctly identifies 'SET key value' from a raw byte stream.

tasks.description: Implement a network listener that accepts multiple concurrent TCP connections and spawns a goroutine for each.
tasks.success_criteria: Server can maintain 5 idle client connections simultaneously.

tasks.description: Link the TCP listener and the parser to the storage engine to perform real data operations.
tasks.success_criteria: Sending 'SET name gemini' via telnet allows a subsequent 'GET name' to return 'gemini'.

tasks.description: Write a background worker that appends every write command to a 'log.aof' file for crash recovery.
tasks.success_criteria: Restarting the server restores the previous state from the .aof file.

goal: Master Database Indexing with B-Trees and Hash Indexes
description: Daily study and implementation of various indexing strategies to understand how they speed up data retrieval and impact write performance.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during the final hour of the workday.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires visualizing tree balancing; recommended to use a whiteboard.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must compare Index Scan vs. Sequential Scan for every test case.

tasks.description: Identify a slow query in a local database and create a B-Tree index on the filtering column.
tasks.success_criteria: The execution plan (EXPLAIN) shows the database is now using the index.
tasks.fallback_version: Read a documentation page on B-Tree internals.

tasks.description: Draw the insertion of 5 keys into a B-Tree of degree 3, showing the splits and parent promotions.
tasks.success_criteria: The final tree satisfies all B-Tree properties (balanced, correct key distribution).
tasks.fallback_version: Use an online B-Tree visualizer to watch 5 insertions.

goal: Build a Document-Store Mock from Scratch
description: Create a Python-based mock of a document database (like MongoDB) that stores JSON-like objects and supports basic filtering.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use Python 3.x. Data stored as local JSON files.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must support nested field querying (e.g., 'user.profile.name').

tasks.description: Write the logic to write and read dictionaries to individual files named by their unique ID.
tasks.success_criteria: A document can be saved to disk and reloaded into memory as a Python dict.

tasks.description: Write a function that iterates through all documents and returns those matching a specific key-value pair.
tasks.success_criteria: The query find({'status': 'active'}) returns only correct documents.

tasks.description: Implement a recursive dot-notation parser to allow querying deep nested objects.
tasks.success_criteria: The query find({'author.id': 5}) correctly retrieves the nested match.

goal: Weekly Database Migration Practice
description: Maintain the ability to safely evolve database schemas without data loss by practicing migrations weekly.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Sunday morning sessions. Using PostgreSQL.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires managing local dev/prod environments.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must include a 'Down' script for every migration to revert changes.

tasks.description: Identify a necessary change (e.g., adding a 'not null' constraint or splitting a column) to a test table.
tasks.success_criteria: A migration plan is documented on paper.
tasks.fallback_version: Use a standard 'Add CreatedAt column' task.

tasks.description: Develop the 'Up' and 'Down' SQL scripts for the change.
tasks.success_criteria: Scripts are tested locally and show the correct table modification.
tasks.fallback_version: Write only the 'Up' script.

tasks.description: Verify that all existing data rows are correctly transformed and no data is truncated.
tasks.success_criteria: A 'SELECT COUNT(*)' check matches before and after the migration.
tasks.fallback_version: Manually check 3 sample rows.

goal: Configure a High-Availability PostgreSQL Cluster
description: Set up a Primary-Replica architecture to understand streaming replication and failover mechanisms.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use two local Docker containers to represent nodes.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires understanding network bridging between containers.
baseline.non_negotiables: Replica must be in 'Hot Standby' mode, allowing read-only queries.

tasks.description: Enable WAL (Write Ahead Log) archiving and create a replication user with appropriate permissions.
tasks.success_criteria: The primary container starts and logs show it is ready for replication connections.

tasks.description: Use the 'pg_basebackup' tool to copy the primary's data directory to the replica's location.
tasks.success_criteria: The replica's data directory is populated with a direct copy of the primary.

tasks.description: Configure the replica to point to the primary's IP and start the recovery process.
tasks.success_criteria: The primary's 'pg_stat_replication' view shows the replica in the 'streaming' state.

tasks.description: Insert a row into the primary and immediately query the replica to see the data.
tasks.success_criteria: The row appears on the replica with sub-second latency.

goal: Build a Simple Relational Database Engine in Python
description: Create a minimal storage engine that saves data in a CSV-like format but supports SQL-style filtering and table scans.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must not use any external database libraries. Data must persist in local .txt files.
baseline.physical_constraints: Memory management is handled by Python's garbage collector, but large scans may cause lag.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must support the 'SELECT * FROM table WHERE column = value' operation.

tasks.description: Create a schema definition file that stores column names and data types (e.g., id:int, name:string).
tasks.success_criteria: A 'metadata.json' exists that correctly describes the target table structure.

tasks.description: Write a function to convert a Python list into a comma-separated string for disk storage.
tasks.success_criteria: Input [1, 'Alice'] results in the string '1,Alice\n' in the storage file.

tasks.description: Write a function that reads the file line-by-line and yields rows as lists.
tasks.success_criteria: Function returns a list of all 10 pre-inserted rows from the .txt file.

tasks.description: Add logic to the table scan to return only rows where a specific index matches a target value.
tasks.success_criteria: Searching for ID=5 returns exactly one row with that ID.

goal: Daily Review of Database Sharding and Partitioning Strategies
description: Study the differences between horizontal sharding, vertical sharding, and range-based partitioning through daily conceptual drills.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during the morning coffee break (15 mins).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to explain the 'Hotspot' problem for each strategy.

tasks.description: Sketch a diagram of how User IDs 1-1000 would be distributed across 3 shards using Modulo (ID % 3).
tasks.success_criteria: A visual mapping of at least 5 IDs to their respective shards is completed.
tasks.fallback_version: Read the Wikipedia summary of 'Database Shard'.

tasks.description: Explain out loud the impact of a specific partitioning strategy on cross-shard joins.
tasks.success_criteria: The primary disadvantage (e.g., increased complexity for global queries) is identified.
tasks.fallback_version: Write a one-sentence summary of the disadvantage.

goal: Deploy a Redis Cluster for Distributed Caching
description: Configure three Redis instances to act as a cluster with data sharding to understand horizontal scaling.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Docker Compose for local orchestration.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires at least 2GB of free system RAM.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must verify that keys are automatically assigned to different slots/nodes.

tasks.description: Create 3 redis.conf files, each enabling 'cluster-enabled yes' and specifying a unique port.
tasks.success_criteria: Three config files exist with correct cluster parameters.

tasks.description: Use the 'redis-cli --cluster create' command to link the 3 containers into a single logical unit.
tasks.success_criteria: Command output shows 'All 16384 slots covered'.

tasks.description: Set 10 different keys and use 'cluster nodes' to see which node stores each key.
tasks.success_criteria: Keys are distributed across at least 2 out of the 3 nodes.

goal: Weekly Audit of Slow Query Logs
description: Identify and optimize the worst-performing queries in a personal or staging database environment.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Saturday morning review (30 mins).
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires access to the server's file system or administrative DB views.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must address at least one 'Top 5' slow query every session.

tasks.description: Query the `pg_stat_statements` table or read the `slow_query_log` file to find queries with the highest total time.
tasks.success_criteria: A list of the top 3 slowest queries is generated.
tasks.fallback_version: Review the most frequent query instead of the slowest.

tasks.description: Run `EXPLAIN` on the slowest query and determine if an index or query rewrite is needed.
tasks.success_criteria: An optimization action (e.g., 'Add index on order_date') is recorded.
tasks.fallback_version: Read a blog post on SQL performance tuning.

goal: Implement a Vector Database Mock for Semantic Search
description: Build a tool that stores text embeddings and performs 'K-Nearest Neighbors' (KNN) search using Cosine Similarity.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python with NumPy.
baseline.physical_constraints: Cosine similarity math is computationally expensive for large lists.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use a dot product or cosine distance formula.

tasks.description: Write a function that takes two arrays and returns a score between -1 and 1 representing their similarity.
tasks.success_criteria: Function returns 1.0 for two identical vectors.

tasks.description: Create an in-memory dictionary to store IDs as keys and their corresponding 128-dimensional vectors as values.
tasks.success_criteria: The collection can hold and retrieve a vector by its ID.

tasks.description: Write a loop that calculates the similarity between a query vector and every vector in the collection.
tasks.success_criteria: The function returns the Top 3 IDs with the highest similarity scores.

goal: Master Recursive Common Table Expressions (CTEs)
description: Learn to use recursive SQL queries to traverse hierarchical data structures like organizational charts or file systems.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use PostgreSQL or SQL Server.
baseline.physical_constraints: Recursion limit errors can crash the session if not careful.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must include a termination condition (WHERE clause) to prevent infinite loops.

tasks.description: Create an `employees` table with `id`, `name`, and `manager_id` columns, inserting a 3-level deep hierarchy.
tasks.success_criteria: Table is populated with at least 10 rows representing a tree structure.

tasks.description: Draft the initial SELECT statement that retrieves the top-level manager (where manager_id is NULL).
tasks.success_criteria: Query returns the single root node of the hierarchy.

tasks.description: Add the `UNION ALL` and the second SELECT statement that joins the CTE to the `employees` table.
tasks.success_criteria: The query successfully returns all employees with their hierarchy level (depth).

tasks.description: Enhance the query to construct a 'path' string (e.g., 'CEO -> VP -> Manager') for each row.
tasks.success_criteria: Output includes a readable path column showing the reporting chain.

goal: Build a Recommendation Engine with Neo4j (Graph Database)
description: Model user-product interactions as a graph and write Cypher queries to find 'Products purchased by others who bought X'.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Neo4j Desktop or Sandbox.
baseline.physical_constraints: Graph visualization requires a monitor, not suitable for mobile.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the `MATCH` and `RETURN` clauses effectively.

tasks.description: Sketch the nodes (Person, Product) and relationships (BOUGHT, RATED) on a whiteboard.
tasks.success_criteria: A visual diagram defines the labels and relationship types.

tasks.description: Write `CREATE` statements to insert 5 users, 10 products, and 15 purchase relationships.
tasks.success_criteria: Executing `MATCH (n) RETURN n` shows the connected graph.

tasks.description: Write a Cypher query to find products bought by people who also bought 'Product A'.
tasks.success_criteria: The query returns a ranked list of recommended products based on purchase overlap.

goal: Perform Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR) in PostgreSQL
description: Simulate a catastrophic data loss (e.g., accidental DROP TABLE) and restore the database to the state exactly before the error occurred.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Requires a local Postgres instance with WAL archiving enabled.
baseline.physical_constraints: High stress scenario; follow steps methodically.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must verify data integrity after the restore.

tasks.description: Configure `postgresql.conf` to set `archive_mode = on` and specify an `archive_command` to copy logs to a backup folder.
tasks.success_criteria: Server restarts successfully and WAL files appear in the backup directory.

tasks.description: Run `pg_basebackup` to create a foundational snapshot of the database.
tasks.success_criteria: A complete data directory copy exists in the backup location.

tasks.description: Insert a timestamped 'marker' row, wait 1 minute, then DROP the table. Note the time of the DROP.
tasks.success_criteria: The table is confirmed missing from the active database.

tasks.description: Stop the server, replace the data directory with the base backup, configure `recovery.signal` with the timestamp target, and restart.
tasks.success_criteria: The server starts up, and the table exists containing the 'marker' row but not the DROP command.

goal: Daily CAP Theorem Analysis
description: Build intuition for distributed database trade-offs by analyzing one system daily and classifying it as CP (Consistency/Partition Tolerance) or AP (Availability/Partition Tolerance).

baseline.fixed_constraints: Morning study routine (10 mins).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must identify what happens during a network partition for the chosen system.

tasks.description: Choose a database (e.g., Cassandra, HBase, MongoDB, DynamoDB) to analyze.
tasks.success_criteria: Target system is selected.
tasks.fallback_version: Re-analyze 'standard' MySQL Replication.

tasks.description: Research default behavior: Does it stop accepting writes (CP) or accept writes that might conflict (AP) when nodes lose contact?
tasks.success_criteria: The system is correctly classified as CP or AP.
tasks.fallback_version: Read the 'Architecture' section of the official docs.

goal: Implement a Database Connection Pool from Scratch
description: Create a resource manager that maintains a set of open database connections to reduce the overhead of establishing new TCP handshakes.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python or Java.
baseline.physical_constraints: Concurrency management is key (locks/semaphores).
baseline.non_negotiables: Must implement `acquire()` and `release()` methods.

tasks.description: Create a class that opens N connections on startup and stores them in a thread-safe queue.
tasks.success_criteria: The queue contains N valid connection objects.

tasks.description: Write the `acquire` method that pops a connection from the queue, blocking if empty until a timeout.
tasks.success_criteria: Calling acquire returns a valid connection; calling it N+1 times blocks.

tasks.description: Write the `release` method that returns a connection to the queue and notifies waiting threads.
tasks.success_criteria: Releasing a connection allows a blocked `acquire` call to proceed.

tasks.description: Add a check (e.g., 'SELECT 1') before handing out a connection to ensure it hasn't timed out.
tasks.success_criteria: Broken connections are discarded and replaced automatically.

goal: Implement the Zettelkasten Method for Knowledge Management
description: Set up a digital slip-box (Zettelkasten) using Obsidian to interconnect ideas and improve long-term retention of research.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Obsidian or Roam Research.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must distinguish between 'Fleeting Notes', 'Literature Notes', and 'Permanent Notes'.

tasks.description: Install Obsidian and create three top-level folders: 'Inbox' (Fleeting), 'References' (Literature), and 'Slipbox' (Permanent).
tasks.success_criteria: The folder structure is visible in the file explorer.

tasks.description: Read one article and summarize it in a single note within 'References', including the source link/citation.
tasks.success_criteria: A note exists containing a summary in my own words.

tasks.description: Identify 3 distinct atomic ideas from the literature note and create 3 separate files in 'Slipbox'.
tasks.success_criteria: Three new files exist, each containing one single idea.

tasks.description: Use wikilinks (e.g., [[Note Name]]) to connect the new permanent notes to each other or to the index note.
tasks.success_criteria: Graph view shows connections between the new nodes.

goal: Daily Spaced Repetition System (SRS) Maintenance
description: Commit to clearing the Anki review queue every single day to maximize the benefits of the forgetting curve.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during morning transit or coffee.
baseline.physical_constraints: Mental fatigue prevents doing >100 cards/day; adjust settings if overwhelmed.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must answer honestly; do not hit 'Easy' just to clear the queue.

tasks.description: Complete all cards scheduled for today in the main deck.
tasks.success_criteria: The 'Due' counter reads 0.
tasks.fallback_version: Review only 10 cards to maintain the streak.

tasks.description: Identify any card marked as 'Again' more than twice in the session and flag it for rewriting.
tasks.success_criteria: Difficult cards are flagged or suspended.
tasks.fallback_version: Ignore leeches for one day.

tasks.description: Create 5 new cards based on things learned yesterday.
tasks.success_criteria: 5 new cards are added to the 'New' queue.
tasks.fallback_version: Add 0 new cards if the review queue was >50.

goal: Learn to Touch Type at 80 WPM
description: Retrain muscle memory to type without looking at the keyboard, prioritizing accuracy over speed initially.

baseline.fixed_constraints: 15 minutes of practice before starting work.
baseline.physical_constraints: Ergonomic posture required to prevent RSI.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the correct finger for every key; no 'hunting and pecking'.

tasks.description: Spend 2 minutes practicing 'asdf jkl;' drills to center the hands.
tasks.success_criteria: Warmup completed with >95% accuracy.
tasks.fallback_version: Rest hands on home row and visualize key positions.

tasks.description: Complete one lesson on Keybr.com or Monkeytype focusing on current problem keys.
tasks.success_criteria: Lesson completed with 100% accuracy (speed irrelevant).
tasks.fallback_version: Type a single paragraph from a book slowly.

tasks.description: Take one 60-second typing test to measure current WPM.
tasks.success_criteria: WPM result is logged in a spreadsheet.
tasks.fallback_version: Skip the test if hands are tired.

goal: Master Mind Mapping for Brainstorming
description: Learn and apply the rules of effective mind mapping (radial structure, keywords, colors) to deconstruct a complex topic.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use blank paper and colored pens (analog preferred for learning).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use a central image. Must use curved branches.

tasks.description: Draw the main topic (e.g., 'Health') in the center of the landscape page using 3 different colors.
tasks.success_criteria: A central image exists with no text outside of it yet.

tasks.description: Draw 5-7 thick branches radiating from the center, each representing a major sub-category (e.g., Diet, Sleep, Exercise).
tasks.success_criteria: Branches are curved and labeled with a single keyword.

tasks.description: Add thinner branches to each main branch to elaborate on details (e.g., Diet -> Veggies -> Spinach).
tasks.success_criteria: At least 3 levels of hierarchy are established.

tasks.description: Add small icons or sketches next to at least 5 keywords to trigger visual memory.
tasks.success_criteria: The map contains at least 5 supporting illustrations.

goal: Weekly Feynman Technique Session
description: Identify gaps in knowledge by attempting to explain a complex concept in simple language.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Friday afternoon review.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use analogies. Must identify where the explanation breaks down.

tasks.description: Choose one topic learned this week that feels 'fuzzy' or difficult.
tasks.success_criteria: Topic title written at top of a blank page.
tasks.fallback_version: Pick a random topic from the course syllabus.

tasks.description: Write an explanation of the concept as if teaching it to a 12-year-old, avoiding jargon.
tasks.success_criteria: At least 2 paragraphs are written.
tasks.fallback_version: Explain it out loud to an empty chair.

tasks.description: Review the explanation and highlight areas where you used complex words to mask a lack of understanding.
tasks.success_criteria: At least one 'knowledge gap' is circled.
tasks.fallback_version: Compare explanation with the textbook definition.

tasks.description: Go back to the source material to clarify the specific gap identified in Task 3.
tasks.success_criteria: The gap is filled and the explanation is re-written.
tasks.fallback_version: Read the specific paragraph in the book again.
